Expressões em contexto (14)

  • come on vamos lá / qual é
    “Um, they will say, "Come on, just spit it out.”
  • figure out entender / desvendar
    “Something that is a headscratcher is something that is hard to figure out.”
  • come up with bolar / inventar
    “I'm always trying to come up with different names now.”
  • feel like estar a fim / parecer
    “And sometimes, I think in the moment when something happens, you feel like, oh, I could never drop this.”
  • kind of meio que / tipo
    “And so, and you have to use that special um comb and a special kind of shampoo.”
  • for sure com certeza
    “Yeah, for sure.”
  • even though embora
    “Even though you're not really doing it.”
  • you know sabe / entende
    “It's It goes around with kids, you know, like sharing hair brushes in school.”
  • i mean quer dizer
    “I mean, I think a lot of sports rules.”
  • i guess acho que
    “I guess hockey is a little more straightforward than football, but I still don't know all the rules.”
  • a couple of alguns / um par de
    “Yeah, I think I had a couple of bouts of it as a kid.”
  • used to costumava
    “What was the name that you used to use in like every roleplay for a while?”
  • have to ter que
    “And so, and you have to use that special um comb and a special kind of shampoo.”
  • want to querer
    “I want to build my own furniture, but the directions are a real head scratcher.”

What do we need to do in order to start thinking in English? And that is what we are diving into today. I'm excited. Yes.

Thank you for letting us know. Yes. It says, "But I find it challenging to speak English and connect with other people in an unnatural and spontaneous way. I mean, I'm always having to prepare for a long time to speak.

I mean, this is one of the biggest issues ESL speakers face in English and it's a huge part of connection. Exactly. Because when you're speaking spontaneously, you're not just talking. You are reacting to the other person or people if it's a group, expressing yourself, joking, keeping the conversation alive and building that connection.

That's a lot to think about. And constantly stopping to form sentences or think about what you want to say can make all of that very difficult. Yeah. Exactly.

So, I mean, speaking spontaneously is really tricky, Aubrey. I mean, it's all about what's going on in your brain. And if you're translating, for example, from your first language, how quickly you can think of the words that you need. So, what happens is you're translating and this ultimately slows you down.

And not only that, we talk about this a lot on the podcast. It slows you down and it causes errors that you wouldn't make if you are instead observing English and thinking in English. Because when we translate between languages, the grammar is always different. The vocabulary is always different and we make these mistakes when we're expecting it to work differently in our first language and in English and it often does not.

Yeah, exactly. So today we are going to give you tips on how to think in English. Right. So this can speed up the process and you're going to start to remember vocab faster.

This takes time and consistent practice, but you can speed up the process with some really good tricks. Yeah, I love these. So, the first trick, guys, is to narrate your activities in English. This sounds it it I'm, you know, I'm sure some listeners are hearing this and they're thinking it sounds a little bit cheesy, right?

Or it might feel cheesy the first time you try it, especially if it's out loud. If you're like, I'm walking from my bed to the door, and now I'm opening the door. It might feel strange, but this is making your brain think those things in English without first thinking it in your first language. Exactly.

And what's great is you can do this alone, you know, just while getting ready in the morning. Okay. You know, oh, I'm I have to brush my teeth. Um, let me make breakfast.

Um, yeah. And even all the steps of that, I'm going to brush my teeth. I'm opening the tube of toothpaste. I'm squeezing it and putting some on my toothbrush.

Now I'm Maybe don't talk while you're brushing your teeth. You're going to make a mess. But then think in your mind. Now I'm brushing my teeth with my mouth as closed as possible so I don't make a mess.

This is what I tell my children. Like why why is there toothpaste dots all over this mirror? Um but even yeah, if you're walking somewhere or talking to pets, that's a really great one. Um people talk to their pets all the time.

So give that a try. That's just as much as you can say as possible. And you don't have to be with a human person speaking English in order to do this. Exactly.

This is because that's one of the hardest things sometimes is finding that conversation partner, those those speaking partners. This can either be in your head if you're on the subway or somewhere in public, just saying it in your head and then out loud whenever you're alone. And you can if you're walking to the park, if you're on a run, if you're alone at home, do this out loud. describe what you're doing as you're cooking out loud and then you will start to recognize vocabulary gaps.

If you want to say, "I'm using a spatula to flip this egg and you don't know how to say spatula," look it up, right? Figure out, use your vocabulary notebook and add to your vocabulary so that you can describe what you're doing. Yeah. Exactly.

So, this might sound like, you know, oh, I'm running late or where did my hairbrush go? Or, wow, this food smells good. Right. This is just um anything anything that's happening.

And I love all of those points because it's not just I'm doing this and then I'm doing this and I'm using this to do it. It's also how am I feeling? What am I smelling? What am I missing?

What am I thinking? Right? There's a lot that you would think in your first language. I'm feeling tired or maybe plans for later like oh I can't forget to call you know Stacy later or something like that.

Exactly. Think that in English. And don't do this all day long. you will be exhausted.

Like 30 minutes a day, take 30 minutes to really focus and have all of your thoughts be in English and narrate what you're doing in English. And then maybe if you don't feel too exhausted, do it again later in the day for another 30 minutes. Right. And so this is going to start to, you know, as Aubrey said, you turning it into active vocabulary, you're going to start to just get more comfortable with getting the words out.

So removing some of that spatula might be on in your vocab note notebook somewhere. Maybe it's passive vocabulary that you have learned but you haven't actually used it in a conversation. And clearly it's not in my active vocabulary yet because I can't say it right now. But if I kept narrating my thoughts, eventually I would be able to, right? And I love what you said about like finding the gaps. I mean, that's a that's huge, right?

So, just you will find gaps. Wait, I actually don't know how to say that. And so, you're going to find different ways to learn and you're going to be practicing while you're relaxed. So, that's huge as well.

Yes, this will remove that fear of speaking or at least a lot of it because it will feel more natural. It's low stress. You're building confidence by yourself as you narrate your thoughts and start thinking in English. Hey, quick question.

Do you want to feel more confident when you speak English? If so, we made a free guide for you. It'll help you avoid those common mistakes and start speaking more naturally in real conversations. Go grab it in the description below.

So, this means to learn common phrases, chunks we often call them, used by natives. These are not just one word responses. It's a whole chunk. They are go-to phrases that are related to new vocabulary words.

And that when you're thinking in those whole chunks, those whole phrases, trains your brain to think in English. Yeah. So, this might sound like instead of saying nervous, saying I'm pretty nervous about or what else are right. Yes.

Or instead of saying just funny, you might say that's so funny. That's the funniest thing I've ever heard. And as you're observing English, shadowing English. Add these words in the chunk they were used by the native speaker to your vocabulary notebook.

And then practice when you are thinking in English, use the whole phrase. Yes, exactly. um or busy like I've been really busy lately. I can't I can't believe how busy uh this this week has gotten something like super busy.

I have such a busy crazy week, right? These chunks where we would use that word instead of just I am busy, right? Yes. Use these more native chunks and that's yeah this is how native speakers think, right?

This is how you think in your first language and you need to do it in English as well. It will make you sound more natural. It's kind of a shortcut. There's no need to construct a whole new sentence because you've learned the vocabulary as part of a chunk.

Yeah. Exactly. And the third thing is let go of perfection. And I bet you're not surprised to hear us say that because we believe in connection, not perfection.

Yeah. This reminds me of our roast. If you guys heard the roast, all of us AI is like, you guys talk about connection, not perfection a lot. It's because we really believe it.

AI. True. Get off our books. Yeah.

Exactly. Leave us alone. Um Yeah. So, you know, um you know, respond before you feel ready and start your sentence and fill in the words as you go.

So, you don't have to wait to create a full sentence in your head. And then again, accept the mistakes. We're all going to make mistakes. And just realize it's part of the process.

Expressões em contexto (8)

  • hold on espera / aguenta
    “Hold on.”
  • look like parecer
    “34 minutos e 4 segundos I know what this must look like.”
  • kind of meio que / tipo
    “Speaking of what kind of law do you practice?”
  • you know sabe / entende
    “You know what?”
  • i guess acho que
    “Better late than never, I guess.”
  • right now agora mesmo
    “I can't take your call right now.”
  • have to ter que
    “10 minutos e 27 segundos I have to go back home.”
  • want to querer
    “I want to say I'm sorry.”

Expressões em contexto (4)

  • come on vamos lá / qual é
    “Come on.”
  • hold on espera / aguenta
    “Hold on.”
  • i guess acho que
    “I guess.”
  • want to querer
    “So, do you want to go back?”

making a phone call dialogue one hello Jane this is Dad oh hi Dad what are you doing I just thought I'd call to see how you're doing I'm doing okay but I can't talk long right now I'm sorry is this not a good time I'm just about to go out with some friends to dinner it's good to hear you're making friends I know I'm really happy here when is it a good time to call back just call me about this time tomorrow I should be here okay I'll do that have fun tonight I will it was good to hear from you okay I'll call you tomorrow I love you love you too Dad bye bye dialogue 2 thank you for calling Joe's Pizza how may I help you yes can I place an order for delivery yes you may go right ahead I'd like two medium pizzas both with olives and ham is that all I'd also like a 2 L bottle of coke would you like any bread sticks sure that'll be fine your total comes to $17.80 can I have your address 1480 popler Drive how long will it take it should be there within 45 minutes thanks thank you for calling Joe's good night dialogue three hello yes is that ivy this is Professor Smith oh hi professor this is a surprise I just called to see if you wanted to come over for dinner this Saturday night really I'd love to what's the reason I'm just having a few students over for dinner since it's the end of the year what a great idea it's just to celebrate the end of the school year yes I do this every year for some of my students great should I bring anything no just bring yourself okay what time should I be there come here around 7:00 do you know how to get here I think so it's just north of you want to buy the car well I'd like to take a look at it it's a 1978 Corvette right that's right it's in good shape I just put new brakes on it how how much are you asking for it I was looking for you know where King Street is yeah it's right behind the stadium that's right just go to the end of the street last house on your left great I'll see you then dialogue five this is 9 911 emergency how may I help you yeah there's been a car accident just outside my house can you tell me where you are located I live at 1411 tree Avenue A car just turned over can you tell me what happened yeah this car comes around the turn too fast doesn't stop in time hits a street sign and then flips over are there any other cars involved no just this one has anyone come out of the car no no one's come out I can't see inside the car should I go check no stay where you are are help will be there shortly can I have your name yeah it's Ben Sullivan thank you Mr Sullivan just wait until an officer arrives making appointments dialogue one can I help you yes is this Dr Fred's office it is is there something I can help you with yes I need to make an appointment well let me see when the doctor is available this is just a routine checkup it's not urgent how about sometime next week that should be fine I've got an opening on Wednesday and Thursday both in the afternoon Thursday should be good okay I'll set it up I have one more question yes do I need to bring any medical records that might be helpful all right I'll be here next week dialogue 2 hello hello can I help you yes I wanted to get a quick trim I'm sorry we're full today would you like to come back another time that would be nice I recommend that you make an appointment what days do you have available would you like to come in tomorrow yes I would like that very much we have two times open 2:30 and 4:30 in the afternoon I'd prefer the 4:30 time okay I'll write that down here try to come a little early is there anything else I need to know yes here is your appointment card bring that with you when you come in great I guess I'll see you tomorrow see you then have a nice day bye-bye dialogue three hi there what can I do for you yes I just need to tune up on my car and maybe a new battery what kind of car is it it's a 1998 Volkswagen Jetta how long have you had a battery in it I've had the current battery for about 3 years yes it's time to get a new battery all right then can I get that done here yes but we're full today you'll have to come back another time okay can I make an appointment for tomorrow sure what time do you want to come in anytime in the afternoon would be fine how about 3:00 that would be great I'll see you then dialogue four hello Mr Taylor can I ask you a favor sure Ken what do you need I had some questions about our class there are some things that I'm having trouble with like what for example it's kind of complicated I wanted to set up a time to meet with you I see when would you like to meet any afternoon this week I'm teaching most afternoons how about Friday Friday afternoon is good for me well then shall we meet at 4:00 yes that sounds good where shall we meet just in the student center okay I'll see you then I'll see you then Ken dialogue five hello is this Dr Cooper's office yes it is what can I do for you my dog needs some shots we can do that here what kind of dog do you have he's a German Shepherd he's about a year old what day would you like to come in sometime early next week would be best for me how about next Tuesday morning that would be fine I'll just need some information fill out this form please do you have a pen there's one on the desk have you been here before no a friend recommended this place to me in that case you may want to sign up for our new customer plan yes I see that on the form here I think I will sign up for that plan just let me know when you're finished talking about daily activities dialogue one where are you going I'm going to the store I need to buy some new razor blades at this hour it's almost midnight I know but I have a job interview tomorrow I'm out of sharp razors I guess you don't want to make a bad impression with a bad shave exactly to be honest I hate shaving every day I can't sympathize I don't have to shave every day some guys don't they're lucky I have to or I start to look like a bear maybe you should let yourself look like a bear very funny what kind of job or girlfriend would I get then that's true but then you wouldn't have to shave every day some things are worth the trouble as long as you're happy I'm happy anyway you'd better before the store closes right I'll be back in a little bit dialogue two look at the time it's 5 minutes past 9 I guess I'm late for work again I hate to ask but why are you late so often if you must know it's because I usually take too long eating breakfast really I almost never eat breakfast especially during the week I don't see how you do that I have to eat breakfast every day why is it a habit for for you no if I don't eat breakfast then I don't have enough energy during the day I guess I'm lucky that I don't have that problem yes you do do you notice how tired you get later in the morning that's when I drink coffee to wake me up that works but it's artificial you're not really getting any real energy maybe but I'm not late for work either work is not the most important part of life it is for me let's just say that breakfast is a daily habit for you and coffee is a daily habit for me breakfast is not a habit it's a healthy way to live I'd love to talk about this more but I've got to get back to work we can talk more over coffee later dialogue three did you check your email today no I didn't have time why our friend Steve just got a new job in Chicago that's great by the way do you check your email every day every morning and night it's a habit I like to stay informed what are you afraid of missing I doubt your life is that exciting very funny if I'm already on the internet why not take an extra minute to look for new email I don't because half of my email is just advertisements it's garbage mine too but I still like to know what's going on well then I can count on you to tell me what's happening with our friends don't worry I won't let you down on that job I know that's what I think is so funny dialogue 4 I thought I saw you out running this morning you probably did I was out jogging that's good do you go every morning I try to unless the weather's bad or I'm not feeling well really I never feel well if that means jogging at Sunrise does that mean you don't enjoy running it certainly does especially so early I understand that it's hard to get out of bed in the morning A lot of times then why do you do it it's become a habit I don't feel awake in the morning unless I go running first I guess I could see how that might be true but I'd rather get a bit more sleep I have to get up out of bed and start to get dressed before I can think about it the alarm goes off and you get up immediately yes immediately otherwise I'll realize how tired I feel and just fall back asleep what about when it's cold in the morning like any other activity I just wear warmer clothes I guess that makes sense maybe you should come with me some time maybe not you have fun I'll sleep late dialogue five what are you doing now I'm going home to water my plants do you have to do that every day of course don't you need water every day I hadn't thought of it like that do you have to do anything else to take care of them not really I just have to give them plenty of sunlight and some plant food that's not too much work but isn't it hard to do it every day sometimes it's not convenient but it's easier than taking care of a child what does that have to do with the subject one day I want a child if I can't take care of a plant how can I take care of a child so the plant is just practice for raising a child no not really it's just something I tell myself in order to be responsible oh I see well I'd better not keep you from your plants you can come with me and see what I have to do okay this could be fun maybe May I'll learn something too maybe so let's go talking about past activities dialogue one it's really cold out today I know it kind of reminds me of that trip we took with Mom and Dad to Canada you mean the one we took when we were very young yes you may have been too young to remember no I remember the trip I don't remember how cold it was though that's because mom had you wrapped up very warm it was cold though I guess so I wonder why she didn't keep you as warm maybe she didn't love you as much you're not funny anyway I wanted to play in the snow we don't have much snow here in Florida that's why a day so unusually cold like this reminds me of that trip where exactly did we go in Canada we went to Toronto and Newland we stayed in a cabin out there that's right I remember the cabin it was very nice maybe I should take my family on a vacation like that your kids might enjoy it yes they would they haven't seen snow yet let me know what you plan to do I will I certainly will dialogue two that was a good basketball game today yeah it was I actually felt young again it was probably one of my best games since high school yeah it seemed like everything just worked right today do you remember that game we played against Lincoln High School do you mean the one in our senior year how could I possibly forget that was our best game ever I wish I was that good now at basketball me too but I guess that's a part of getting older what's a part of getting older accepting that you're not young anymore maybe so but that game we played today for our company made me feel young enough you're right remember what we used to do after a game back then yes I do we'd steal beers from your dad's refrigerator and drink them well we're too old to steal let's go get some beers though good idea you lead the way dialogue three do you want to go to the beach with me next weekend sure are you driving is that a problem I'm a good driver I hope you're better than you were in college what are you talking about college was 10 years ago you don't remember when you borrowed your your dad's car on Christmas that's right I almost forgot I drove it into a mailbox I don't know how you forgot your dad was so upset I know that was the last time I ever drove his car remember he thought you had been drinking yes he couldn't believe that I was sober and driving that bad why did you hit that mailbox anyway I just wasn't paying attention I'd only been driving for a few months I hope you're a better driver now if you're driving to the beach of course I'm better now after after that accident I had to get better okay I trust you to drive next weekend good but I didn't say that I wanted to drive don't look at me you're the good driver now remember dialogue 4 this is a pretty nice restaurant you found I know it's one of the best Italian restaurants in the city that reminds me of this kid I knew in elementary school how I have to hear this story there was one kid who could eat spaghetti noodles through his nose that's disgusting how did he do that I guess it's easy he'd just inhale a long noodle through his nose and then down into his throat that's really disgusting I'm surprised he didn't get sick sometimes he did have trouble with that trick what happened he'd sneeze and the noodle would fly back onto the plate that story almost ruined my dinner why did you tell me that you asked me to tell you remind me next time not to ask to hear one of your stories before I eat okay that's fair anyway let's eat I'm starving I am too although I'm not as hungry as I was a few seconds earlier by the way that kid was myself I should have known you're very talented just don't do that at dinner tonight I promise not to dialogue five hey we need to take another trip like we did in college which which one the trip to Europe yes of course that was a good time that's not practical we have families and jobs now we can't just go away whenever we want maybe not but it's still fun to think about remember when you had to sell some of your clothes in Germany because you ran out of money I didn't run out of money my wallet was stolen that's right on the bus in Berlin yes and then later I found that extra money in the bottom of my suitcase it was nice of your father to pack that in there for you I think it was just his way of showing how much smarter he is than me he's probably right yes he probably is right anyway maybe we could plan a trip to Europe again take the wives and kids with us do you really think so it would be a lot of work yes but it would be worth it I guess you'd have to stay away from the girls this time though yes I definitely would I'll talk to my wife about a trip and see what she thinks I'll do the same this could be a lot of fun talking about future activities dialogue one I think we're going to save some extra money this year is that because of the tax cuts that's right do you think we should spend that money or keep saving it I think we should take a nice vacation really we just went to see your parents for Thanksgiving you know what I mean I think we should take a real vacation not just going to see relatives where would you like to go a friend of mine at work just got back from the Bahamas we could go there that would be nice do you think the kids would be happy there I'm sure they would we could find out more though would you mind going somewhere with more Wilderness areas like what for example I've always wanted to go to Alaska maybe do some fishing and Hiking I could do that but I'd prefer to lie on the beach and get some sun well we've got plenty of time to plan even the planning will be fun I can't wait to get started dialogue 2 What language class did you sign up for what what do you mean our school requires everyone to study a language oh right I signed up for Spanish you want to take Spanish this year why it's becoming a very common language in this country it just makes sense to understand Spanish that's true but it's kind of boring I'm a business major I don't care about languages what language are you going to study me I'm going to study Japanese wow that's difficult I know but I figured it would be interesting it sure will be but don't complain to me when it gets difficult then don't complain to me when Spanish gets boring it will be boring but I don't care well I'm looking forward to studying Japanese keep me updated about how it's going dialogue 3 what are you doing later this week I don't know why why do you ask maybe we could go to the art museum for our class project what class project you don't remember the professor in our art history class said we have to each make a trip to the art museum and write about it oh yes I completely forgot what day do you want to go I was thinking about Friday afternoon after class that sounds good to me what do you want to write about I heard that the museum has some pieces by Rodan I was going to write on that who is Rodan he's a famous French sculptor he did the statue called The Thinker I've seen that one that would be interesting to write about that statue isn't there but there are others we'll have to go see them at least that gets this requirement done and out of the way I agree I hate waiting until the last moment dialogue 4 it's hard to believe we're going to graduate in 2 months I know time has passed by so fast are you still planning to go to medical school yeah I'm ready to go what about you I'm going to take a year to go work in Chicago then try to decide that sounds like a good idea what kind of job in Expressões em contexto (11)

  • come on vamos lá / qual é
    “Okay, come on.”
  • go on continuar / prosseguir
    “Do you want to go on a date?”
  • point out apontar / destacar
    “You say this to point out someone is the same.”
  • hold on espera / aguenta
    “Hold on.”
  • no way de jeito nenhum
    “There's no way out.”
  • at least pelo menos
    “You say, "I'll tell you this much." Like at least this is true.”
  • you know sabe / entende
    “You know, you don't have to explain.”
  • i mean quer dizer
    “I mean, I don't know if I feel comfortable.”
  • right now agora mesmo
    “You say, "Is now a good time to talk?" Like, "Can we talk right now?" Dr.”
  • have to ter que
    “You don't have to explain.”
  • want to querer
    “Do you want to go on a date?”

Diálogo — conversa real (315 falas)

Introduction & Hook: Tom's Embarrassing Childhood Story Hi friends, I'm Lily and this is the podcast where we speak real English together every single day. And I'm Tom. If you are new here, welcome. We are so happy you found us.

Tom, we have new members. Ah, there it is. That's the news I was hoping for today. It just made my day.

The newest members, welcome to the family. We're so happy to have you with us. Welcome, guys. If today first time here, please subscribe.

We upload a new English lesson every day. Okay, today's topic is one of my favorite topics ever. We are talking about childhood, growing up, being a little kid. Yes, the good times, the embarrassing times, the times you still think about and go, "Why did I do that?

I think Tom has a few of those. A few, maybe more than a few. Today, you will learn vocabulary about childhood, a really useful grammar point, and you will hear some stories from me and Tom. Some of them are funny, some of them are a little bit embarrassing.

Mine are embarrassing. Okay. But first, I have a question for you. Stay with us.

So Tom is going to tell us a story later in this episode. A story about something that happened when he was 7 years old at a school sports day. I can't believe we're doing this. He ran a race and something went wrong.

Real English Conversation: Growing Up, Childhood Games & Favorite Foods Something that everyone saw. All the parents, all the teachers, all the kids. Please stop. Here is the question.

What do you think happened to Tom at that race? Write your answer in the comments right now. Tell us your guess. We want to read every single one.

I already regret this episode. Okay, let's get into the lesson. Tom, let's talk about when we were kids. Where did you grow up?

I grew up in a small town. Really small. One main street, one park, one ice cream shop that was only open in summer. That sounds like a film.

It felt like a film. Everyone knew everyone. If you did something silly at school, your mom knew about it before you got home. That is so true.

In small towns where I grew up, it was a bit bigger, a city. But my neighborhood was small and quiet. We always played outside after school. What did you play?

Everything. We played tag. We played hideand seek. We drew on the pavement with chalk.

In summer, we stayed outside until it was dark. Oh, that sounds really nice. It was. I also had a bicycle, a pink one with a white basket on the front.

I loved that bicycle so much. I had a bicycle, too. Mine was blue. I rode it everywhere down the hill near my house, which looking back was probably not a safe idea.

How fast did you go? Very fast. Too fast. I crashed into a bush once.

Just went straight into it. Disappeared completely. Did anyone see? My neighbor.

She was in her garden. She just looked at me and said, "Are you okay, Tom?" Very calm, like it happened every day. It probably did, effing every day. Yeah, it probably did.

Okay, so we both had bicycles. We both played outside. We both grew up in neighborhoods where people knew us. But tell me one thing.

What was your favorite food as a kid? Oh, easy. Cereal. I would eat cereal for every meal if my parents let me.

For dinner. For breakfast, for a snack, anytime. I loved it. That is not a meal, Tom.

I know that now. I didn't know it then. My favorite food was my grandmother's rice. She made it every Sunday.

We always went to her house on Sundays. The whole family. It was loud and warm and the food was always amazing. That sounds lovely.

I miss those Sundays. You don't really understand how special they are when you're a kid. You just think, "Oh, Sunday again. Grandma's house again." And then you grow up and you would give anything to have one more of those days.

Yeah, I know exactly what you mean. Okay, let's learn some vocabulary so we can all talk about our childhoods in English. Let's go. All right, before the vocabulary, let's talk about grammar.

And today's grammar point is a really important one for talking about the past. We are looking at used to. Yes, used to. This is how we talk about things that happened regularly in the past but don't happen now.

Things that were normal for us as children but are not normal now. So, for example, when I was a kid, I used to eat cereal for dinner. Great example. And I used to ride my pink bicycle every day after school.

The structure is simple. Subject plus used to plus the base verb. I used to "Used To" Grammar Explained: Talking About Past Habits in English play. She used to sing.

They used to walk to school. And for negative sentences, we say didn't used to without the d at the end of used. I didn't used to like vegetables. He didn't used to wake up early.

And for questions, did you used to? Exactly. Did you used to play outside? Did she have a pet?

This is one of those grammar points that sounds a little strange the first time, but you get used to it very quickly. Get used to it. That's a different phrase. Oh no, I didn't mean to do that.

Okay, so just to be clear, used to do something is about the past. Get used to something means you become comfortable with something. They look similar, but they are different. We will save get used to for another episode.

Today we are just practicing used to for childhood memories and we will use it a lot in the next section. Okay. Childhood vocabulary. Let's go Tom.

Word number one. Grow up. This means to change from a child to an adult. I grew up in a small town.

She grew up with three brothers. Where did you grow up? And you can use used to with it in a different way. When I was growing up, I used to love cartoons.

Beautiful. Now, Tom, story time. Where did you grow up? Tell us something about it that you haven't told us yet.

Okay, so I grew up near a small river. Not a big river, a tiny river. More like a wide puddle, really. A wide puddle.

But to me, it was huge. I used to go there every weekend with my dad. We used to take a little bag of bread and feed the ducks. Oh, that is so sweet.

There was one duck we called Gerald. Big duck, very confident. He used to come right up to us and just stare at us. Not even waiting for bread, just staring.

Gerald sounds intense. He was. I think he was in charge of the other ducks. Okay, word number two, best friend.

Your best friend is the friend you are closest to, the one you tell everything to. My best friend's name was Sarah. I used to walk to school with my best friend every morning. We are still best friends Childhood Vocabulary in English: Grow Up, Best Friend, Playground, Pocket Money, School Trip today.

Did you have a best friend when you were little, Lily? Yes. Her name was Mia. We met on the first day of school.

I remember I was really nervous and she just walked up to me and said, "Do you want to be my friend?" Just like that. No nervousness at all. That's so cute. We used to do everything together.

We used to have sleepovers every Friday. We used to stay up all night talking and laughing. Her mom would knock on the door at midnight and say, "Girls, please sleep." And we would stop for about 5 minutes and then start again immediately. Word number three, playground.

The playground. The place at school where children play during breaks. swings, slides, climbing frames. We used to play football on the playground.

There was a big slide on our playground. I loved the playground when I was a kid. Tom, what do you remember about your school playground? Oh, we had this one climbing frame.

It was metal and in summer it got really hot in the sun and every single year someone touched it with their bare hands and went ow. And we all laughed. And then the next year a new kid did the same thing. It was always someone new every year, like a tradition.

Also, we had a tuck shop, a little window where you could buy sweets during break. I used to spend all my pocket money there on Fridays. All of it. Every single penny gone by Friday lunch.

The weekend was just waiting. Word number four, pocket money. Pocket money. The small amount of money that parents give children.

I used to get pocket money every week. I saved my pocket money for months to buy a toy. He always spent his pocket money on sweets like someone we know. I don't know who you're talking about.

I used to save my pocket money. I was very careful with it. I had a little ceramic pig, a piggy bank, and I used to put my coins in it every week. What were you saving for?

A camera. A small camera for kids. I saw it in a shop window and I just had to have it. I saved for about four months and when I finally had enough, my dad took me to the shop and I bought it myself.

I carried the bag home like I had just bought a house. That is the best feeling when you buy something with your own money for the first time. It really is. I took photos of everything for a month.

The garden, the cat, my shoes, everything. Word number five, school trip. A school trip. When a whole class goes somewhere together for the day.

A museum, a farm, a park, somewhere interesting. We had a school trip to the science museum. I remember our school trip to the farm. I used to love school trips because you didn't have lessons.

That was the main reason, wasn't it? Mostly. Yes. Okay.

So, speaking of school trips, this is the story. The one from the beginning. Oh, the sports day story. Not a school trip.

It was sports day. Okay. So, every year our school had a sports day. Parents came, teachers came, everyone watched.

I was seven and I was in the running race, the 50 meter sprint and I was at the start line. I was very focused, very serious. The teacher said, "Ready." I was ready. The teacher said, "Set." I was set.

And then she said, "Go." And I ran. I ran as fast as I have ever run in my life. And the wrong way. I ran the wrong direction.

Completely the wrong way. All the other kids went that way and I went that way. By the time I understood what happened, I was just standing there alone and all the parents were looking at me. Tom, I still don't know how it happened.

I just ran the wrong way. What did you do? I turned around and finished the race. I came last by quite a long time, but I finished.

That is amazing. I love that you finished. I finished. That's the important thing.

Okay, so now you know the story. Go back Tom's Sports Day Story: What Really Happened to your comment. Was your guess right? Write it below.

We are going to read every single comment. Please be kind. Okay, let's go through everything we learned today. Quick summary.

Grammar used to for things that happened regularly in the past. I used to play outside every day. She used to wake up early. They used to walk to school together.

And the negative is didn't used to. I didn't used to like vegetables. Questions. Did you used to vocabulary word number one, grow up?

I grew up in a small town. Word number two, best friend. My best friend and I used to talk all night. Word number three, playground.

We used to play on the playground every break. Word number four, pocket money. I used to save my pocket money for weeks. Word number five, school trip.

I used to love school trips because there were no lessons. Word number six, sports day. Our school sports day was every June. Lesson Summary & Speaking Practice with "Used To" I remember mine very well.

We know, Tom. We all know now. Okay, here is your question for today. And we really, really want you to answer this one.

Tell us one memory from your childhood. It can be funny, happy, embarrassing, anything. Use the words from today if you can. Use used to if you can.

For example, when I was a child, I used to play football with my friends every day. Or my best friend and I used to build blanket forts in the living room. Write it in the comments. We read them.

We reply. We love hearing about your lives. And if you want to practice more, try writing three sentences about your childhood with used to. It is really good practice.

Okay, that is everything for today. Thank you so much for being here. Every single one of you, 53,000 of you and more every day. We are so grateful.

If you are not subscribed yet, please hit that button now. We upload a new lesson every single day. So there is always something new waiting for you here. And come back tomorrow.

We will be here. Same time, new lesson, new story. Maybe one of mine won't be embarrassing next time. Maybe.

See you tomorrow, Daily English Talk family. Keep practicing, keep going, and keep speaking English every day. Bye.

Expressões em contexto (5)

  • grow up crescer
    “Where did you grow up?”
  • you know sabe / entende
    “Okay, so now you know the story.”
  • right now agora mesmo
    “Write your answer in the comments right now.”
  • used to costumava
    “We are looking at used to.”
  • want to querer
    “We want to read every single one.”

Frases prontas (40)

  • If you are new here, welcome.
  • We are so happy you found us.
  • Tom, we have new members.
  • Ah, there it is.
  • That's the news I was hoping for today.
  • It just made my day.
  • The newest members, welcome to the family.
  • We're so happy to have you with us.
  • If today first time here, please subscribe.
  • We upload a new English lesson every day.
  • Okay, today's topic is one of my favorite topics ever.
  • We are talking about childhood, growing up, being a little kid.
  • I think Tom has a few of those.
  • A few, maybe more than a few.
  • Some of them are funny, some of them are a little bit embarrassing.
  • But first, I have a question for you.
  • So Tom is going to tell us a story later in this episode.
  • I can't believe we're doing this.
  • He ran a race and something went wrong.
  • Real English Conversation: Growing Up, Childhood Games & Favorite Foods Something that everyone saw.
  • All the parents, all the teachers, all the kids.
  • Here is the question.
  • What do you think happened to Tom at that race?
  • Write your answer in the comments right now.
  • Tell us your guess.
  • We want to read every single one.
  • I already regret this episode.
  • Okay, let's get into the lesson.
  • Tom, let's talk about when we were kids.
  • Where did you grow up?
  • I grew up in a small town.
  • That sounds like a film.
  • It felt like a film.
  • That is so true.
  • In small towns where I grew up, it was a bit bigger, a city.
  • But my neighborhood was small and quiet.
  • We always played outside after school.
  • What did you play?
  • We played hideand seek.
  • We drew on the pavement with chalk.

my arms be careful not to bump your elbows she has delicate hands with long fingers keep her fingers crossed for good look my chest felt tight with anxiety the heart is a vital organ for circulation I evident they have a lot of affection for each other after the divorce they maintained a good relationship the separation was difficult for both living together has greatly improved their relationship ensure Financial records are accurate and taxes are paid properly lawyers defend the rights of individuals and business businesses salespersons connect customers with products that meet their needs project managers oversee projects for conception to completion marketing professionals craft strategies to engage potential customers sales professionals excel in closing deals and expanding business opportunities construction workers build the infrastructure of our communities drivers transport goods and people safely to their destinations Farmers grow the food that feeds the nation cooks and chfs Delight patrons with culinary Creations store attendance and Cashers facilitate a pleasant shopping experience cleaning professionals maintain clean and healthy environments machine operators run and maintain Machinery in various Industries electricians ensure our homes and businesses have power plumbers address and fix issues with water and drainage systems mechanics keep vehicles and creamy pasta is quick and easy to prepare honey is sweet and natural nuts are great for a snack oysters are a good source of zinc eggs are great for breakfast fish are good for heart health popcorn is in the morning Sushi is a delicious way to eat fish tomatoes are juicy and red the floral dress is perfect for summer I prefer sneakers for daily Comfort a Blazer can transform any look the mid card is trendy this year the white T-shirt is an essential basic tailored trousers bring Elegance to the office the leather jacket is a wardrobe classic flats are ideal for a work day the sweatshirt has become a sweatwear fashion piece Jans are the all season staple a well-cut shirt is essential for the Modern Man shorts are perfect for hot summer days a heavy coat is necessary for the harsh winter the Overcoat is the key piece for winter looks leggings are comfortable for exercise and Leisure scar pun add Elegance to any dress boots are essential for fall and winter a hat can add personality to the look a wool scarf is great for warming up in Winter colorful socks can be a fun touch a belt can Define the silhouette in a dress the right bag completes any outfit sunglasses are fundamental for sunny days a silver bracelet can be a delicate accessory a necklace can be the focal point of a look large earrings are in fashion this year delicate Rings complement any hand a classic watch is a symbol of style a tie adds formality to man's attire a silk scarf is a versatile accessory suspenders bring a vintage charm leather gloves are elegant and practical in the code sandals are perfect for summer a practical backpack is essential for everyday use the cardigan is a comfortable piece for Mid season polo shirts are a CLE chick option a comfortable pajama is essential for a good night's slip stylish swimsuits are trendy at the beach swim trunks are the classic choice for man at the beach a cap is great for sun protection I visited the library to study I arrived early at the airport climbed a challenging Mountain we watched a movie at the cinema the theater had an exciting play the stadium was full of fans I enrolled at the UN University this year I visited the hospital for a checkup I took the train at the station we took the kids to the zoo we had fun at the amusement park we camped in a quiet Forest we traveled through the desert on camels the Botanical Garden was In Bloom we bought fresh fruits at the market the art gallery was fascinating I work it out at the gym in the morning we visited an ancient Temple the church was decorated for the wedding we explored a historic synagogue the castle had magnificent architecture the palace was simply Splendid we spent a day on the island I saw a Durant volcano we swam near a waterfall we rolled on the lake at dusk they fish it in the calm River we avoided the swamp during the hike the canyon offed spectacular views the lighthouse got Ed the ships they walked on the pier at Sunset we tasted wines at the winery she visited a lavender farm we explored a hidden cave how do I get to the nearest subway station does this bus go to the city center where can I buy a ticket for the train is this the right platform is the subway safe to use at night how long does the trip take by Subway where can I top up my travel card can you tell me when we reach Central Station do I need a special pass for unlimited rides which bus go to the airport from here are there any delays on the subway line today hit the skillet before adding oil use the wooden spoon to stir the sauce make healthy juice in the blender a complete cutlery set is essential for formal dinners serve the main meal on a deep plate fill the glass with ice water the coffee cup should be warm cook the rice in a pot with a lid bake the cake in a greased baking pan mix the dough in the mixer until smooth cut the vegetables on The Cutting Board place a close napkin on your lap the tablecloth should be clean fill the pcture with natural juice add salt from the salt shaker while cooking use the pepper shaker to season the salad open the can with the can opener grate the cheese with a fine grater extract the juice with the manual juicer bake the bread in a preheated oven heat leftovers in the microwave grind coffee beans in the grinder store leftovers in airtight container serve the salad in a large bow place the cooked pasta in the colander peel the potatoes with the peeler open the wine bottle with the car screw wait the ingredients on the kitchen scale measure the milk in a glass measuring cup serve hot soup with the ladle flip the pancake with the spatula use the fork to hold the meat a sharp knife makes cutting vegetables easier steer the tea with a long spoon toast with a glass of red wine arrange the condiments on the tray put fruit in the basket for dessert make lose tea with the tea infuser check the oven temperature with the thermometer protect your hands with the oven meat he's intelligent and always finds Solutions her empathy makes her a loyal friend with Charisma he captivates any audience her friendliness makes her easy to get along with generosity is her trademark honesty is her fundamental principle with patience he faces challenges courage helps her overcome obstacles humility is her most prominent characteristic her sensitivity is evident in her action creativity is her source of innovation determination leads him to his goals kindness is her distinctive trait tolerance makes him respectful to all perseverance is her key to success compassion is what she always shows her loyalty is admired by everyone altruism is her way of life respect forms the basis of her relationships understanding makes communication effective resilience helps him overcome challenges flexibility is her adaptation skill her assertiveness ensures results Elegance is present in her way of being authenticity is her personal brand sincerity is her most valued quality solidarity is her commitment to others curiosity drives her quest for knowledge responsibility is her driving force his sense of humor brightens any situation do you have a table available yes we have a table available can I see the menu here is the menu what's the dish of the day the dish of the day is p do you have vegetarian options yes we have several vegetarian options what do you recommend I recommend the salmon what's the soup of the day the soup of the day is tomato can I order this glutenfree yes we can prepare it gluten-free is this dish spicy it's not spicy can you make this dish dairyfree yes we can make it dairyfree what wines do you have we have a selection of local wines do you have a children's menu yes here's the children's menu can we sit on the Terrace of course follow me can I change the side dish yes you can change it for vegetables does this dish contain nuts no this dish does not contain nuts can I have more bread please sure I'll bring them more bread how long will the order take it will take about 15 minutes can I see the dessert menu here's the dessert menu do you have decaffeinated coffee yes we have decaffeinated coffee okay can I pay by card yes we accept cards can you split the bill yes we can split it can I order to go sure what would you like to take does this dish have meat no it's a vegetarian option do you have any special offers today today we have two for one on cocktails can I have some water please yes I'll bring you water right away can I reserve a table for tomorrow sure what time is this Fish Fresh yes the fish arrived this morning can I have the dressing on the side of course the dressing on the side do you have Wi-Fi yes the password is wifi1 123 where are the bathrooms the bathrooms are at the back to the right can I have the bill please yes I'll bring you the bill right away can this dish be shared between two people yes it's quite large and perfect for share ing what is the difference between these two pastas the main difference is the sauce one is tomato based and the other is cream can you recommend a wine that goes well with my dish sure for this dish I would recommend a smooth red wine is there an ingredient in this dish that's not mentioned on the menu yes this dish has a bit of celery is that a problem is this dessert homemade yes all our desserts are homemade what's the origin of this dish this dish has influence from class Italian cuisine can I order a half portion instead of a full one sorry we only offer fulls size dishes what type of oil do you use for cooking we use extra virgin olive oil for most of our dishes can I have a surprise for the birthday person of course we can prepare a little surprise dessert the dairy products are in io5 please where is the wine session the wine M section is in iio 7 towards the back of the store do you sell cold beer yes it's in the refrigerated section near the entrance can I go to the selfservice checkout sure they located near the front of the store do you accept credit cards yes we also accept cash and debit cards are these eggs free range yes the eggs are from free range chickens how late is the Del section open the Dallas section is open until 900 p.m. where is the Frozen Seafood section Frozen Seafood is in iio 12 near the back can I get a rain check for this item yes I can issue a rain check for you is there a discount on wholesale purchase purchase there is a 10% discount on both purchase over $50 is there any special promotion today yes check our front entrance for today's deals where are the fresh fruits and vegetables located in the produce section at the back do you offer home delivery yes we do you can sign up online or at the checkout can I use discount coupons along with promotions yes for most items unless specify as otherwise how can I sign up for the supermarket loyalty program sign up at the customer service desk or online her sadness was evident in her eyes they share a deep and enduring love for each other English SPEAKING Practice - Let_s say it together!112 falas · 4 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (112 falas)

All right, let's practice speaking English. We got a little text here. Let's see what it's about. Let's see what this is all about.

Last winter, something strange happened to me late one night. All right, feel free to shadow me. Say it along with me. Okay.

I was home alone because my partner was away for work and I'd decided to stay in for the evening. It was a cold, quiet night and the house felt unusually still. It was already pretty late when I decided to go to bed around 11 or maybe closer to midnight. I'd turned off most of the lights and was walking upstairs thinking about my day.

Outside the street was silent. No people, no cars passing by. Outside the street was silent. No people, no cars passing by.

As I reached the top of the stairs, I heard a sound. At first, it was very soft and unclear, like a light tapping or a whisper. I stopped and listened carefully, trying to figure out where it was coming from. The sound seemed to be moving, which immediately made me feel uneasy.

A few seconds later, I heard it again. This time, it was louder and slower, almost like footsteps. A few seconds later, I heard it again. This time, it was louder and slower, almost like footsteps.

It seemed to be coming from the right from right outside near the front door. It seemed to be coming from right outside near the front door. My heart started pounding and I suddenly became very aware that I was completely alone in the house. I stood there for a moment trying to stay calm, telling myself there was probably a simple explanation.

I stood there for a moment, trying to stay calm, trying telling myself there was probably a simple explanation. Maybe someone was just walking past the house. Or maybe it was an animal moving out around outside. Maybe someone was just walking past the house.

Or maybe it was an animal moving around outside. Still, I couldn't shake the uneasy feeling. I couldn't shake. That means to get rid of.

If you're If you shake something off, you get rid of it. I couldn't shake it. Couldn't get rid of it. Make it go away.

I decided to go downstairs and see what was going on. I walked down slowly trying not to make any noise. When I got to the front door, I didn't open it right away. Instead, I went to the window and looked outside.

The street looked perfectly normal. No people, no cars, nothing moving. The street looked perfectly normal. No people, no cars, nothing moving.

The street lights were on and everything was quiet. I started to feel a little silly and told myself I'd probably imagined it. But just as I was about to walk away, I heard the noise again. This time it was much clearer and it really did sound like footsteps right in front of the door.

My stomach tightened and I stood there listening. After a few seconds, I took a deep breath and slowly opened the door. But just kidding. Did I scare you?

Did I make you jump? Outside, sitting calmly on the step was a large black cat. It was wearing a collar with a small bell that jingled whenever it moved. The cat looked at me for a moment, then got up and walked off into the darkness.

I closed the door, locked it, and quietly laughed at myself. I felt relieved, but also a little foolish for getting so scared. Still, before I went to bed, I checked the door twice before finally falling asleep. All right, that was our fun little story.

Let's go ahead and shadow him now. I'm going to say it nonstop, nice, fast, natural pace. See if you can keep up with me. You can just listen or you can shadow me.

Here we go. Last winter, something strange happened to me late one night. I was home alone because my partner was away for work and I decided to stay in for the evening. I was a cold it was a cold, quiet night and the house felt unusually still.

It was already pretty late when I decided to go to bed around 11 or maybe closer to midnight. I'd turned off most of the lights and was walking upstairs, thinking about my day. Outside, the street was silent. No people, no cars passing by.

As I reached the top of the stairs, I heard a sound. At first, it was very soft and unclear, like a light tapping or a whisper. I stopped and listened carefully, trying to figure out where it was coming from. The sound seemed to be moving, which immediately made me feel uneasy.

A few seconds later, I heard it again. This time, it was louder and slower, almost like footsteps. It seemed to be coming from right outside near the front door. My heart started pounding and I suddenly became very aware that I was completely alone in the house.

I stood there for a moment trying to stay calm, telling myself there was probably a simple explanation. Maybe someone was just walking past the house. Or maybe it was an animal moving around outside. Still, I couldn't shake the uneasy feeling.

I decided to go downstairs and see what was going on. I walked down slowly, trying not to make any noise. When I got to the front door, I didn't open it right away. Instead, I went to the window and looked outside.

The street looked perfectly normal. No people, no cars, nothing moving. The street lights were on and everything was quiet. I started to feel a little silly and told myself I'd probably imagined it.

But just as I was about to walk away, I heard the noise again. This time it was much clearer, and it really did sound like footsteps right in front of the door. My stomach tightened, and I stood there listening. After a few seconds, I took a deep breath and slowly opened the door.

Outside, sitting calmly on the step, was a large black cat. It was wearing a collar with a small bell that jingled whenever it moved. The cat looked at me for a moment, then got up and walked off into the darkness. I closed the door, locked it, and quietly laughed at myself.

I felt relieved, but also a little foolish for getting so scared. Still, before I went to bed, I checked the door twice before finally falling asleep. Boom. Good workout.

Way to go. Thank you for practicing with me again today. Keep moving forward. Take it one step at a time, and I'll see you in the next practice video.

Listen, Learn, Repeat & Shadow English83 falas · 3 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (83 falas)

Are you ready to listen to the instruction manual? I prepared a little instruction manual for you today. This is going to be mostly a listening exercise, but you can you can learn, repeat, and shadow with me. Do the best you can.

I'm going to go fast. I'm going to go fast, and I'm not going to pause much. So, here we go. You ready?

Do you know why so many people study English for years but still struggle to speak with confidence? It's not because they're lazy. It's not because they're weak. It's because they spend all their time training their mind but not their mouth.

Think about it. You memorize grammar rules. You read books. You get good scores on exams.

But when it's time to actually speak, your tongue feels heavy. Your mind goes blank and your confidence quietly disappears. Here's the simple truth. To speak English naturally and confidently, you have to train your mouth, not just your mind.

Imagine this. You didn't learn to ride a bicycle by reading an instruction manual. You didn't learn to ride a bicycle by reading an instruction manual, right? You learned by getting on the bike, losing your balance, falling, trying again, and slowly moving forward.

Speaking English works the same way. It's not about how much English you know, it's about how much English your mouth has practiced saying. Today, I'm going to show you step by step how to train your mouth, not just your mind, so you can finally speak English naturally, fluently, and with real confidence. Does that sound good?

All right, stay with me because by the end of this lesson, you'll not only understand the secret, you'll also have powerful daily speaking exercises that can transform your English faster than you ever imagined. All right, first let's uncover the biggest mistake most English learners make. Many students believe fluency comes from knowledge. But fluency doesn't live only in your brain.

More vocabulary does not equal fluency. Fluency lives in the muscles of your mouth, your lips, your tongue, your jaw, your voice. Speaking is a physical skill as much as a mental one. When you train your mouth, you build muscle memory.

Just like a singer warms up before a performance or an athlete practices movements before a game. You need to practice speaking until your mouth moves smoothly without hesitation. Now, think about children. How do they learn their first language?

They repeat. They imitate. They repeat sounds over and over until speaking feels natural. The same secret works for you.

If you want to speak English confidently, stop waiting for the perfect sentence in your head. Instead, start saying small, simple sentences out loud again and again and again until your mouth feels relaxed and comfortable. Now, let's get practical. If you truly want to train your mouth, you need daily speaking exercises.

Use the shadowing technique. Listen carefully and repeat after me right away. Copy my rhythm. Copy my inonation.

Don't overthink it. Your goal is imitation, not perfection. If you do this every day, you'll feel your mouth becoming more flexible, just like an athlete stretching their muscles. You might be thinking, "But what if I feel awkward or nervous when I speak?" That's normal.

Every learner feels this way. Confidence is not something you wait for. Confidence is something you build. Here's the truth.

Your brain may still think in your native language, but your mouth is learning a new rhythm. So when you speak, accept small mistakes. Don't fight them. Don't stop in the middle of a sentence.

Keep going. Because confidence is not perfection. Confidence is the courage to continue. Every mistake is proof that you're training your mouth.

And with every word you say out loud, you're building a stronger, more natural English voice. Real fluency comes from training your mouth, not just your mind. Every single day, give your mouth the workout it needs. Shadow native speakers, repeat powerful daily sentences.

This small daily habit builds muscle memory and muscle memory creates natural fluency. Now imagine yourself in the future. You walk into a room, someone asks you a question in English and instead of freezing, your mouth responds smoothly, naturally and confidently. No fear, no hesitation, just communication that feels easy and natural.

That is the power of training your English speakaking voice. That is the key to speaking English naturally. So don't just learn English, live it, speak it, and feel it flow. All right, that's it.

Good workout. Having some fun with my power presentation skills there. Keep moving forward one step at a time.

  • have to ter que
    “To speak English naturally and confidently, you have to train your mouth, not just your mind.”
  • want to querer
    “If you want to speak English confidently, stop waiting for the perfect sentence in your head.”

On the path to success, you will find many resting spots, comfortable places where you might feel tempted to stop. And also many dead ends where a plan simply doesn't work.

Remember this is a English speaking practice. Sometimes you guys judge me on the text and not on the practice. So, I had to remind you just now we are practicing English and now we are going to practice shadowing nonstop. We're going to say the text.

Let's say it together. I'll keep a nice pace for you to follow along and say it with me. Are you ready? Here we go.

to find meaning and stability in our lives. That is to feel that life has purpose and is not constantly changing in a scary way. There's a deep psychological truth we need to face. We have to believe that something is more important than just ourselves.

In other words, we need to feel connected to a purpose or cause that is bigger than our own individual wants and needs. This always requires a leap of faith, which means making a choice to trust or believe in something even when we don't have complete proof or certainty. Whether it's religion, something spiritual, family, close relationships, or a career, each of us eventually reaches a moment where we have to make a real commitment. A commitment is a serious promise to ourselves or to others that we will stay with something and keep trying even when it's difficult.

Often we have to make this promise without knowing for sure if it's the perfect or right choice. That's where faith becomes so important. By faith, we don't only mean religious belief. We also mean the willingness to trust that our choices can lead to something meaningful.

Even when the future is uncertain or confusing. In this sense, faith is part of every important decision we make. In the end, nothing in life is completely secular, meaning totally separate from belief or deeper values. All of our commitments, whether to people, work, or ideas, are in their own way acts of faith.

We decide to care, to invest our time and energy, and to keep going even though we can never be 100% sure of the outcome. That quiet act of trusting and continuing is what gives our lives structure, direction, and a sense of real meaning. Boom. Good workout.

Way to go. Way to exercise those English speaking muscles with me again today. Keep moving forward one step at a time. I'll see you in the next English speaking practice

It hadn't been the smooth, relaxed morning I'd imagined. I What's the What's the contraction? I had imagined. So, it hadn't been It had not been the smooth, relaxed morning.

I had imagined. I'd imagined, but at least I made it to work. somewhat prepared. Kind of prepared.

It hadn't been the smooth, relaxed morning I'd imagined, but at least I made it to work somewhat prepared and in one piece. All right, that is the text.

And now we are going to do a nonstop shadow time. Are you ready? Take a breath. All right, here we go.

Yesterday morning, I woke up late because I had accidentally turned off my alarm instead of just hitting snooze like I usually do. I shot out of bed, threw on some clothes, and rushed out of the house without even grabbing breakfast. I was already annoyed with myself, but I kept telling myself I could still make it on time if everything else went smoothly. When I finally reached the bus stop, I saw the bus already pulling away.

I waved at the driver, hoping he might see me and stop, but he just kept going. That's when the frustration really hit me. It felt like the day was determined to work against me. Trying not to panic, I took a deep breath and came up with another plan.

I quickly called a friend and asked if she could pick me up. Thankfully, she was nearby and she said she could swing by. While I waited, I dug through my bag to get organized for the day and realized I had left my headphones at home, too. At that point, it honestly felt like everything was going wrong at once.

I just stood there for a moment, wondering what else could possibly go off the rails. Luckily, my friend showed up just in time, and I climbed into her car, feeling both relieved and a little frazzled. As we drove toward the office, I tried to calm down, focus on my breathing, and go over my notes for an upcoming meeting. I kept hoping my boss wouldn't notice how chaotic my morning had been.

By the time we pulled into the parking lot, I had finally started to feel more grounded and ready to get on with my day. It hadn't been the smooth, relaxed morning I'd imagined, but at least I made it to work somewhat prepared and in one piece. And that's what counts, right? Boom.

Good workout. Way to go. Keep moving forward. Keep practicing.

You can do it one step at a time. I'll see you in the next training

Just LISTEN or SHADOW_ Repeat after me or Say it with me_ Practice Speaking English180 falas · 2 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (180 falas)

Are you ready to listen, learn, repeat, and shadow me? Study and recall memory. Recall something. All right.

So, today we will look at some practical techniques to improve focus and concentration. Study once and recall without struggle. We all want that, right? Okay.

So feel free to repeat after me during this lesson or shadow me. Say it together with me. There is a level of learning where you read something once truly grasp it. If you grasp something, you understand it and later recall it with calm confidence.

No tension in your chest, no blank mind, no heavy sigh as you try to force the answer out. That level is not magic. It is built with intention and mental discipline. Most people read passively.

Their eyes move across the page, but their minds drift. Their eyes move across the page, but their mind drifts. I got an S there. Their mind drifts.

They reach the end of a chapter and realize they cannot clearly explain what they just read. I've experienced that. I reach the end of a chapter and realize I cannot clearly explain what I just read. Then they assume they need to read it again and again.

The real problem was never repetition. The problem was a lack of depth in the first contact. If you want to study once and recall without struggle, you must treat the first exposure as serious work. The first time you encounter information, give it full presence.

Sit with an alert posture. Remove background noise. Make the moment focused and deliberate. Tell yourself clearly that this is not casual reading.

This is construction of memory. This is Oh, this is the construction of memory. I left out the as you move through the material. Do not aim to cover large amounts quickly.

Aim to understand small sections completely. After a paragraph or a key point, pause and mentally summarize. Ask yourself what the core message is. If you cannot say it in simple language, go back and clarify immediately.

Clarity during the first contact reduces the need for repetition later. Engage your senses while learning. Speak important points softly to yourself or speak them out loud if you can. I recommend out loud.

Write brief notes in your own words rather than copying lines directly. Good advice. The act of rewriting forces processing. Processing builds strong memory traces.

Traces. A tra that would be like a memory path. A path in your memory. Copying builds weak familiarity.

That one's hard to say, huh? Familiarity. Copying builds weak familiarity. Emotion strengthens retention.

That's why I try to make my lessons emotional so that you can retain the information. When you encounter an idea that feels important, allow yourself to react internally. If you react internally, you feel it emotionally. Feel the re relevance.

The the relevance is the connection, the importance. Feel the relevance. Notice how it connects to your goals. When knowledge touches your personal ambitions, your brain assigns it a higher priority.

Use visualization with precision. That just means clearly visualize things. If the material involves steps or processes, imagine yourself performing them. Picture the sequence clearly.

See where each part fits. Mental rehearsal makes recall smoother because your mind has already walked through the path. Avoid multitasking during your first study session. Divided attention leads to fragmented memory.

Fragmented means broken up into pieces. Your memory is broken into pieces. Fragmented memory creates struggle during recall. One task at a time, one concept at a time.

Depth over speed. After you finish a focused session, close your material and test your memory immediately. Do not wait. Speak the key ideas aloud.

Write them without looking. The slight effort required to retrieve the information strengthens your ability to recall it later. Retrieval practice builds confidence. Retrieval that's just a recall to remember it.

Remembering practice. Recall practice. retrieval. Your breathing and physical state influences performance.

If you are tense, your recall feels blocked. Before studying, take slow deep breath and release tightness in your shoulders and jaw. A calm body supports a calm mind. Calm recall feels natural.

Remove the habit of rereading without thinking. Rereading gives the illusion of progress. Active engagement creates real progress. When you interact with the content during the first session, you reduce future struggle.

Confidence also shapes recall. If you constantly tell yourself that you forget easily, you program hesitation. Replace that belief with a stronger one. You are capable of retaining what you understand clearly.

Yes, you are. You are. You are capable of retaining remembering what you understand clearly. Speak that belief internally while you study.

Your mindset influences your performance. Limit the amount of material per session. When you overload your mind, recall becomes messy. Choose a reasonable portion.

Master it then move forward. Master in smaller units. Mastery in smaller units builds overall strength. Respect breaks.

Use them wisely. After a strong study session, take a short pause without filling your mind with random content. Give your brain a quiet moment to settle. Silence helps consolidation.

Link new information to real situations. If you learn a principle, think about where it applies in your life. The more practical the connection, the easier the recall. Abstract ideas fade faster than applied ones.

Protect your first study session from negative self-t talk. If you catch yourself thinking, "This is too hard," interrupt that thought. Replace it with patience. Difficulty does not mean inability.

It means growth. When you approach material with steady patience, recall becomes smoother. Train your mind to expect success. Before recalling, pause briefly and assume you can retrieve the answer.

That expectation reduces mental pressure. Pressure often blocks access to stored information. Confidence opens it. At the end of the day, sorry, at the end of your session, create a brief mental review without looking.

Walk through the main ideas in order. Notice if any part feels unclear, address it immediately. Address it means do something about it. Leaving weak spots increases the struggle later.

Sleep supports recall powerfully. After studying for a while with deep focus, allow yourself proper rest. During sleep, your brain organizes what you learned. The next day, briefly test yourself again.

You will notice greater ease. Avoid comparing your pace to others. Focus on your own process. When when you honor your rhythm, memory strengthens naturally.

That was bad. Let me say it again. When you honor your rhythm, memory strengthens naturally. There you go.

Build a habit of short follow-up checks in the days after studying. Even a few minutes of real of of quick recall builds strength. Even a few minutes of quick recall builds strength. This light reinforcement removes the fear of forgetting.

Study once with intention, recall with calm assurance. Calm assurance. This approach changes your relationship with learning. You no longer depend on endless repetition.

You depend on the quality of attention. When you treat your first encounter with information as serious work, your brain responds with respect. It stores more. It organizes.

It organizes better. It retrieves faster over time. And this method builds your self-rust. You sit down knowing that one strong session is enough to understand deeply.

You walk away confident that the knowledge is accessible. That confidence reduces anxiety. Reduced anxiety improves recall. Improved recall strengthens belief.

This cycle builds intellectual resilience. You are capable of learning with strength and recalling without struggle. Yes, you are. This is the last slide.

So, I want you to say this with me. Are you ready? You are capable of learning with strength and recalling without struggle. You can do it.

Boom. Good workout. Keep moving forward one step at a time. Thank you for practicing with me again today and I'll see you in the next training video.
