Repeat-After-Me or just LISTEN or for English Speaking Fluency SHADOWING Practice215 falas · 2 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (215 falas)

All right. Are you ready to win the morning? Let's do it. Now, we're going to listen to English.

7 segundos We're going to learn some English and we're going to learn some life skills along with repeating. 13 segundos Now, the repeating part, I'm not going to pause a lot, but I will pause a little so you can repeat 20 segundos chunks of speech that you want to remember. Shadowing. If you're an expert English practicer, 27 segundos try to match my pace.

Try to say it with me. All right, here we go. When you're morning, before the world wakes, 39 segundos before the world wakes up, when you're morning, I want you to picture something simple and real. 48 segundos The alarm goes off.

It's still dark. The room is quiet. No messages, no noise, no demands. 1 minuto e 1 segundo In that moment, you are not reacting to life.

You are deciding how you will live it. 1 minuto e 13 segundos That first hour of your day carries more power than most people understand. 1 minuto e 22 segundos It shapes your attitude. It affects your focus.

It even influences how you handle money and how much progress you make. 1 minuto e 39 segundos If you lose your morning, you spend the rest of the day trying to catch up. If you win your morning, you spend the rest of your day leading. 1 minuto e 55 segundos Most people wake up and immediately hand their mind over to the world.

Hand over to that's a 2 minutos e 5 segundos phrasal verb. I just highlighted it. To to give to yield control to hand it over. 2 minutos e 13 segundos They grab their phone.

They scroll. They read messages. They read 2 minutos e 23 segundos I put it in t past tense, didn't I? They read messages that create stress.

Oh, they look at other people's lives before they look at their own goals. 2 minutos e 43 segundos Their heart rate goes up. 2 minutos e 48 segundos Their thoughts become scattered. And before their feet touch the floor, they already feel behind.

3 minutos e 1 segundo That is not how a disciplined person lives. Winning your morning starts the night before. 3 minutos e 13 segundos It starts with a decision. You decide what time you will rise.

You decide what you will do in that first hour. 3 minutos e 28 segundos You prepare your clothes. You prepare your workspace. You remove excuses before they appear.

3 minutos e 42 segundos Discipline is not about force. It's about preparation. When the alarm rings, your mind will offer you comfort. 3 minutos e 56 segundos It will say stay in bed.

It will say you deserve more rest. 4 minutos e 5 segundos It will say one more day will not matter. 4 minutos e 11 segundos That voice is normal. Every human has it.

The difference between those who grow and those who stay stuck is simple. 4 minutos e 26 segundos One group listens to comfort. The other group listens to commitment. 4 minutos e 38 segundos Get up when you said you would.

Sit up in your bed. Feel the floor under your feet. 4 minutos e 50 segundos Take a slow breath. Tell yourself clearly why you are awake.

4 minutos e 59 segundos Not in a loud, dramatic way, but in a steady and calm way. 5 minutos e 8 segundos Remind yourself that this day matters. Remind yourself that your future is built in hours like this. 5 minutos e 21 segundos Go wash your face.

Feel the cold water. It wakes your body. It sends a signal that you are alert. 5 minutos e 36 segundos Drink a glass of water.

Your body has been resting for hours. It needs fuel. 5 minutos e 45 segundos These are small actions but small actions done daily create identity. 5 minutos e 55 segundos You start seeing yourself as someone who is serious about life.

Now protect that first 30 minutes of your morning like it's valuable because it is. 6 minutos e 13 segundos Do not check your phone. Do not open social media. Sit in silence for a few minutes.

6 minutos e 22 segundos Notice your thoughts. Are you anxious? Are they negative? Are they hopeful?

6 minutos e 31 segundos You cannot manage your mindset if you never observe it. Write down three things you are grateful for. 6 minutos e 44 segundos simple things, your health, your family, the opportunity to work. 6 minutos e 52 segundos Gratitude steadies your emotions.

It shifts you from pressure to purpose. 7 minutos e 5 segundos A calm mind makes better decisions, especially about money and time. 7 minutos e 14 segundos After gratitude, write down your top priorities for the day. Not 20 tasks, but three to five important actions.

7 minutos e 30 segundos When you limit your focus, you increase your power. you stop reacting to everything and start moving towards something specific. 7 minutos e 46 segundos Your morning is also the best time to strengthen your body. 7 minutos e 54 segundos Move for at least 20 minutes.

Walk, stretch, do push-ups. Feel your muscles working. 8 minutos e 7 segundos Feel your breathing deepen. Physical movement improves your mental clarity.

8 minutos e 18 segundos When your body is strong, your mind becomes more stable. Energy creates confidence. 8 minutos e 27 segundos During this early hour, your mind is clear. It has not been filled with noise.

8 minutos e 38 segundos Use that clarity to learn. Read a few pages of a book that improves your thinking. 8 minutos e 51 segundos Listen to something that challenges you. Not entertainment.

Education. 8 minutos e 59 segundos Your income and your progress grow when your knowledge grows. 9 minutos e 7 segundos The quiet morning is the perfect classroom. Winning your morning is not about waking up at an extreme hour.

9 minutos e 20 segundos It's about waking up with intention. If you rise before the world, you are not rushed. 9 minutos e 33 segundos You are not reacting. You are building.

Consider how your mood changes when you are rushed. 9 minutos e 46 segundos When you wake up late, you hurry. You skip breakfast. You snap at people.

9 minutos e 55 segundos You make quick choices. You spend money on convenience because you feel stressed. 10 minutos e 5 segundos That stress drains your momentum. Now consider a different morning.

You wake up early. 10 minutos e 16 segundos You sit quietly. You move your body. You plan your day.

You step into the world calm and prepared. 10 minutos e 28 segundos Your voice is steady. Your decisions are thoughtful. You say no to things that do not match your goals.

10 minutos e 41 segundos You you that calm focus follows you into your work, into your finances, into your relationships. 10 minutos e 53 segundos The first win of the day builds confidence. It tells your brain that you keep promises to yourself. 11 minutos e 7 segundos Trust.

Self-rust is powerful. When you trust yourself, you take bigger steps. 11 minutos e 17 segundos You invest wisely. You stick to your plans.

11 minutos e 22 segundos You continue when results are slow. Some people say they are not morning people. 11 minutos e 32 segundos I understand that feeling. Habits are strong, but habits can be changed.

11 minutos e 41 segundos Start 15 minutes earlier than usual. Keep it simple. 11 minutos e 48 segundos Consistency matters more than intensity. The early morning also gives you emotional space.

12 minutos e 2 segundos During the day, people need you. Your job demands attention. 12 minutos e 11 segundos Messages arrive. Problems appear.

12 minutos e 16 segundos If you do not create quiet time for yourself, your own goals get pushed aside 12 minutos e 27 segundos and years pass quickly when you live only in reaction. 12 minutos e 35 segundos Winning your morning is an act of self-respect. 12 minutos e 41 segundos It says your growth matters. It says your peace matters.

There will be mornings when you feel tired. 12 minutos e 54 segundos There will be days when motivation is low. On those days, do something small. But do not skip the routine.

13 minutos e 8 segundos Every five minutes of reflection is better than none. Even five minutes of reflection is better than none. 13 minutos e 20 segundos Momentum is fragile. Protect it.

Fragile. Make sure I pronounce that right. Momentum 13 minutos e 30 segundos is fragile. It could be broken.

Protect it. Over time, this early discipline shapes your identity. 13 minutos e 42 segundos You no longer see yourself as someone who struggles to get started. 13 minutos e 49 segundos You see yourself as someone who leads your own life.

That shift in identity changes everything. 14 minutos e 2 segundos Your morning sets the tone for how you think about money. 14 minutos e 9 segundos When you plan your day early, you are more aware of how you spend. 14 minutos e 18 segundos You track your expenses.

You think about long-term goals. You are less likely to make emotional purchases because your mind is steady. 14 minutos e 34 segundos Your morning sets the tone for your mindset. 14 minutos e 40 segundos If you begin with gratitude and focus, you respond to challenges with patience.

14 minutos e 49 segundos You do not panic at every obstacle. You understand that setbacks are part of growth, but they do not define you. 15 minutos e 4 segundos Your morning sets the tone for momentum. When you complete your first planned task before others even wake up, you feel a sense of progress.

15 minutos e 22 segundos That feeling pushes you to complete the next task. Success builds on itself. 15 minutos e 35 segundos There is something powerful about seeing the sun rise while you are already moving forward. 15 minutos e 47 segundos The light slowly fills the room.

The day begins and you are not behind. 15 minutos e 57 segundos You are ahead in mindset, ahead in preparation, ahead in discipline. 16 minutos e 6 segundos People often look for big breakthroughs. They want dramatic change.

16 minutos e 17 segundos Real change is built quietly in hours like these. It is built in calm rooms before the noise starts. 16 minutos e 30 segundos It is built on small choices no one applauds. Win your morning before the world wakes.

And you will notice something. 16 minutos e 44 segundos You feel more in control. You feel less reactive. 16 minutos e 51 segundos You feel stronger inside.

That inner strength carries you through the rest of the day. 17 minutos e 1 segundo Each sunrise gives you another chance not to start over from zero, but to build on yesterday. 17 minutos e 15 segundos Do not waste that chance by handing your mind to distractions. 17 minutos e 24 segundos Rise with purpose.

Sit with clarity. Move with energy. 17 minutos e 34 segundos Plan with focus. Learn with intention.

17 minutos e 39 segundos Step into the day prepared. When you control your morning, you begin to control your life. 17 minutos e 52 segundos When you control your morning, you begin to control your life. And that control earned daily 17 minutos e 59 segundos creates a future that reflects discipline, peace, and steady progress.

And that, my friends, is the 18 minutos e 8 segundos end of our shadowing session for today. Let's say that one more time. And that control earned daily 18 minutos e 15 segundos creates a future that reflects discipline, peace, and steady progress. Boom.

Good workout. 18 minutos e 24 segundos Keep moving forward one step at a time. I'll see you in the next practice video.

Slow English Conversation with my American friend!263 falas · 11 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (263 falas)

Hello everyone and welcome to English Cozy Chat. Today we have a very exciting episode because we are going to have an easy fun conversation with my good friend Kieran. She is on the show today. Actually, it was your idea to come on the show.

I told her I had a YouTube channel and she said I should come on the show and we should do an easy English conversation. So, here we are. She's one of my very good friends and she's also a language learner, so we should have some really fun things to talk about. I think so.

Yeah. Okay. So, hi Karen. Hello.

Meeting Kieran! Tell us a little bit about yourself. My name is Kieran and I moved to Spain three years ago from the United States with my two kids who are almost 8 years old and 6 years old. And my husband is actually from Spain originally.

So that's why we moved here. Cool. Nice. And how do you like it here so far?

I really like living here. It's definitely very different, but it's been a really good experience because of people like Maria. It's actually really funny. We met on Instagram.

That's true. You sent me a message, right? I got like a random Instagram message from someone that I didn't know and she's like, "Hey, do you live in Spain or where we live?" And um I was like, "Yeah." And she said, "I might be moving there." And I was like, "Cool." And we talked like a little bit. And then I didn't hear from you for like a year.

And then I think it was a year later I messaged you and I was like, "Hey, are you moving here in the end?" She was and she was like, "Yeah." And she said, "I'm moving like a kilometer away from where you live like next week." And I was like, "Oh, cool." So it was kind of funny. Then we met and we became friends. The reason I had the Kieran learning Spanish idea for coming on the show today is because I myself am learning how to speak Spanish better. And there's lots of videos online or podcasts where they speak in slow Spanish.

And some of my favorite ones are ones where two people are having a conversation. I didn't know that. I didn't know you watch those. Ah, cool.

Kieran is fluent in Spanish. She speaks very good Spanish. Actually, the just the other day I heard her speak a lot of Spanish and before that I think I hadn't heard you speak in a long time and I was like, "Wow, look at Kieran. She's like really speaking well." So tell us how has your Spanish learning journey been for you and how how has that been for you?

It's been long. uh 2 years before I moved here. So 5 years ago, I started trying to learn Spanish um with a tutor and also through different apps and things like that. And then when we moved here, I also got another tutor and was learning Spanish.

A tutor is a teacher, like a private teacher, right? Yeah. and it was going well, but it was challenging to advance, especially with some of the more specific verbs and changes that happen in Spanish when you're talking about things that happen in the past or in the future. Yeah.

So when I moved here, it was a little challenging for a while because I was very scared to speak in Spanish. I was scared to make mistakes and I was scared people were going to be upset with me making those mistakes. So I didn't use Spanish for a while or I think it was my own fear that prevented that. Yeah.

When I became more comfortable here and started meeting more people, then I started speaking in Spanish more and not really caring if I did make a mistake. And especially speaking in Spanish um with people who who could maybe correct me in the moment as well was helpful. And what I did was find someone who wanted to learn English and they wanted to learn and they and I wanted to learn more Spanish and then we would meet and have conversations where we helped each other and that was really helpful. So I think the biggest obstacle even now is just the fear of speaking right and when I'm calm and able to make mistakes then I learn faster right I think that's everybody's biggest obstacle like an obstacle is like something hard to overcome when learning a language is the fear and the fear of messing up is just in your mind.

It's just in your mind but it has so much control over us. It's true. It will prevent you from doing something that you can really do just cuz you're like I don't want to mess up. I don't want to.

This happens to me still also. And I realize when I forget that I'm speaking the language, that's when I speak the best. It's for example, if I'm meeting a new person and I want to speak well in in Catalan, for example, I will think, oh gosh, what am I going to say? How how do I say it?

And then I'll start speaking and I'm like like it just doesn't come out. But if I like forget that I'm speaking it and I just start it just comes right. Yes. It's all in your head and it just comes with practice, doesn't it?

I think so. It really does. Okay. So, for learning Kieran´s language learning strategies Spanish, what has been your strategy or what has worked for you?

What has helped you to learn? Do you do grammar, uh, vocabulary, listening? I don't know. Do you watch TV or or just speaking with people?

What? I think all of those things. Yeah. One really important thing is listening.

I think I can do that in my own time whether it be like on YouTube like a video like this um or a podcast. And then sometimes I think of how I would respond um in that language. So, sometimes I'll pause the podcast and try to figure out um or the video and try to figure out how I would construct a sentence. Um and then and then going back and and checking if that was correct or not.

And sometimes it's just about learning also like a new word and then challenging yourself to use it in sentences or with friends or a teacher or writing down a sentence and then checking it online to see if it's correct or not. Yeah. And then you feel comfortable using this word and then the next word and the next word and somehow they start to come more easily, especially words that are used in common conversations. Yeah, it's a cool feeling, right?

Like when you hear a word, you don't know what it is, you like look it up, you kind of practice it to yourself, and then in one moment you're like, I'm going to use the word and you say it like while you're speaking, you're like, I'm doing it. I'm like, it's a good feeling. It's a good feeling. But sometimes you can mess up the words.

Embarrassing stories Yeah. Yeah. Of course. So, do you have any funny or embarrassing stories of when you were learning Spanish?

There was one time where I was just learning how to speak Spanish. And remember, my husband is a fluent Spanish speaker. And um I wanted to say, "I'm married." And instead, I said, "I'm tired." Because the words are very similar in Spanish. I'm married is Gaso and tired is Canad.

Ganso. Yeah. So, that was pretty funny. Oh my gosh.

Um, yeah, there can be times like that. And then sometimes you don't realize it. Like in the moment you say something and someone just kind of looks at you like, "Oh." And you're like, "Why are they looking at me like that? I just said I'm married." And then later you realized I didn't say that like I said yeah luckily I did make this mistake with my husband so it wasn't safe space right but um from then on I definitely knew the difference between those two words so it's a benefit to make mistakes.

Yeah. It is. And it's always like a funny a funny memory, right? Yeah.

Especially once you understand it well, you're like, "Oh, remember when I made those mistakes?" Exactly. Back to living in Spain. Are there any Spain vs the US interesting like differences or what what would you say is the biggest difference between Spain and the United States for you? Well, there's lots of little differences, but um one thing that really surprised me when I first came here off the top of my head now that I'm thinking about it is that there are playgrounds and then bars next to the playgrounds.

That's cool, right? Yeah, I think it's really cool. And so it was really nice because I could have a water or a coffee or a glass of wine and then my kids could play on the playground safely. That is that is a good that's a really good one actually because in the US you will never find that like if you are in a um at a place with a playground then there's never going to be like a restaurant beside of it where you can like eat or drink something and watch your kids.

is always like you're at the playground and your kids are playing and you're like here I am wish I could have a coffee watching my kids play again and no I love that I love that out here it's really good yeah so is there anything that you really miss what do you miss about the US I really miss my family and friends but also there's a store in the US called Trader Joe's and I really miss Trader Joe's me too Yeah, Trader Joe's is a store. I think it started in California. They have really inexpensive good products and food. Great snacks.

Fresh products like Great snacks. Every Yeah. Great snacks and great frozen foods. And it's nice when you're a busy family to go ahead and easily grab something that you can make into like a healthy, easy meal.

pretty quickly. I used to get those bean burgers, like black bean burgers. So, they were so good. Like, yeah.

One difference about living here is there's it's not as convenient as the US, which is a good thing because the food is more local and um it's more fresh and you have to cook everything from scratch. But some nights you're tired and it's really hard to find good pre-made items that are available in fame. Yeah. They don't make it easy.

It's like, "Oh, I want like a really fast dinner to just put on the table and and then next thing you know, you're cooking a whole meal because the the the prepared food that you have here is just not very good." But I I guess it's a good thing. It is a good thing. you end up cooking more even though maybe cuz the more you have like prepared convenient food, the more you're going to eat that and it's not as healthy. So, Exactly.

Maybe it's more convenient but not as healthy. Yes, it forces you to slow down. It does. That's one thing about living here and I think in other places in Europe is that it just forces you to slow down a lot more than living in the United States.

It does. I I feel the same way. I feel the same way. Yeah.

Um, okay. Let's move on to your hobbies. Okay. Kieran´s hobbies I'm excited about this one.

Tell us about your hobbies. One of my biggest hobby is that I do ceramics. So, I make ceramics and mostly I make cups and plates and bowls and things that you would use every day in your life. Um, in fact, we have two of my mugs here today that we would have coffee or tea in.

Look at this mug, you guys. Like, how cool. It's like It's not even like a real mug. It's like a little little I don't know.

It's really creative, this little shape that's here. Cheers. I have There's been multiple episodes where I'm like showing everyone. Look at my new mug that I made.

And no, one was a mug that you made me and then I showed an olive bowl and then my the mug that I made. It's been a few things now, but why did you start doing that? I started doing it um when I was pregnant with my first child and I was really wanting to do something like crafty and with my hands and so I started taking classes at a studio, a ceramic studio near my house. And one thing led to another and then I was taking a lot of classes and learning a lot and spending a lot of hours working in the studio.

And even on days when I would try to make a piece and it would fall apart or something happened or it broke, it didn't matter to me. I was still so so happy just to have the experience of making something with my hands. It just brought me a lot of joy. So, it was something I definitely knew I that was important to me and I really wanted to keep doing.

It's something so special, right? Like I have talked about this so many times on the show, how making something with my hands feels and you know how that feels to actually make a mug and to and like every every day when I drink coffee out of the mug that I made like I think I made this. I made this. Like it's so It is fun.

It's Yeah. And because I feel like a lot of us have jobs where we're like at the computer and we are working but it's you don't see a physical result, right? And like with with pottery and ceramics like you get these beautiful results like and same with making bread which I love. and these things where it's like, oh, I worked and like look at this.

It would be the same also, I think, with say woodworking. If you work with your hands or if you're someone who builds um things or you work with iron or um even people who fix things with their hands, you know, like um something is really broken and they fix it, that's also got to be really rewarding. Yeah. Or sewing or knitting.

Yeah. Any of those things. I actually brought this mug today because I don't know if you can see, but it has a very small little like dent right here. Yeah, it has a chip and it's not perfect, but I instead of throwing it away, I decided that it would be important to keep it because it reminds me that um doing this hobby is not for the perfection of it.

It's really for the joy of the making of it. Yeah, that's true. My mug has these like it's it's not straight and it's not flat and it's like, "Oh, but that's perfect for me." Like this imperfect perfect perfect type thing. So, it really makes me happy when um I have other people in the studio and we're working together and um I can kind of have them experience a little of what I experienced when I first started to learn ceramics.

Yeah, she's so good at teaching and it's fun because Thank you. you are good at teaching and like it'll be some of us in there and she does it in the her the garage of her house. She invites people over and we're all making things and we talk and for me it's so much better doing that than just having a coffee with people because you're you're you're working on something together and that's it's really special what you do and I and I also think that is important to also do things to bring bring people together. Yeah.

And I think sometimes we get lazy and we're like, I just be at my house and do my own thing and blah, but if you make a little effort to plan things with friends, to get people together, it always feels good after, doesn't it? It does. And I started I didn't go to school to be doing ceramics. And um I think at points I've thought m maybe I shouldn't be doing this because I wasn't trained professionally in it.

I was trained like on some classes and then a lot of like practice on my own. But then I think then it would just stop you from doing it if everything had to be some professional degree or something. It would just stop you from actually doing the thing that you want to do. So, I say try something you like and keep on doing it.

And even if you don't aren't an expert in it, that doesn't mean that you can't do it and can't enjoy the process. It's such a good It's a really good point. It's actually the same with this YouTube channel because I knew I kind of wanted to do this and I also knew when I started like the it wasn't perfect these videos that I was making like it but it was something I was like just start just create the channel just just do it and then as you go you learn and you're like okay this works this doesn't work and there's also something really beautiful about learning right like and just that whole process process is is really nice. It's about kind of like speaking a new language.

Yeah. Um, are there is there any advice you would have for language learners? For anyone watching the channel, I would say keep watching and and keep trying to to speak. Maybe there's someone in your community or a Facebook group you could join to reach out to talk to someone um in English or whatever language you're learning really. and don't get hard on yourself.

I would say be kind to yourself and keep going and keep trying things that challenge you. As my husband would say, challenge yourself without judgment. Ah, very good. That's great.

Yeah. Yeah, there there are so many ways now that online or like in your community you can find someone who's trying to learn your language that you speak and see if they can do an exchange with you in English. That's a great thing and they just kind of try to look for these resources, right? And I know a lot of people who have had a lot of success in learning by watching shows on television in the language they're trying to learn or listening to shows or podcasts in the language that they're trying to learn and that I think is always a helpful a helpful way.

I know that I watch a lot of Spanish television series and actually it it helps me to kind of learn um the way some of the words are pronounced and the flow of some like sentences that might be different than English. Yeah. And it makes a difference, right? Like if you're watching something that you actually enjoy.

Exactly. And then you're also learning at the same time. That's so important. you don't feel like you're like studying.

And that really does help your brain. When your brain is having fun, it like learns better. It's much easier for it to it's absorb information. Yeah.

And my children speak three languages. Um, and my son when he's speaking in Spanish, because of the fact that he learned Spanish from native Spanish speakers here, he speaks with like the Spanish way of using your hands and the way you would say a sentence. And it's really funny to me because he already has these native mannerisms, ways of saying things that you would only learn from watching a native speaker speak Spanish. English Evolution Step 19 eps
Improve English Practice _ English Grammar Tenses A1.185 falas · 0 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (185 falas)

Every word has a job. Every tense tells a story. With colors, real life examples, and clear scenes, this lesson makes grammar simple, visual, and fun. Let's turn everyday sentences into powerful learning moments.

Go. I will go. I go. I went.

Eat. I will eat. I eat. I ate.

See. I will see. I see. I saw.

I saw run. Run. I will run. I run.

I ran. Read. I will read. I read.

Write. I will write. I write. I wrote.

speak. I will speak. I speak. I spoke.

Listen. I will listen. I listen. I listened.

Play. I will play. I play. I played.

Work. I will work. I work. I worked.

Learn. I will learn. I learn. I learned.

Teach. I will teach. I teach. I taught.

Buy. I will buy. I buy. I bought.

I bought sell. Sell. I will sell. I sell.

I sold. Make make. I will make. I make.

I made. Take. I will take. I take.

I took. I took give. Give. I will give.

I give. I gave. Find. I will find.

I find. I found I found bring bring I will bring I will bring I bring I bring I brought I brought Brought catch. Catch. I will catch.

I catch. I caught. Drive. I will drive.

I drive. I drove. Ride. I will ride.

I ride. I rode. Swim. I will swim.

I swim. I swam. Fly. I will fly.

I fly. I flew. draw. I will draw.

I draw. I drew. Build. I will build.

I build. I built. Cook. I will cook.

I cook. I cooked. I cooked clean. Clean.

I will clean. I clean. I cleaned. wash.

I will wash. I wash. I washed. Fix.

I will fix. I fix. I fixed. Open.

I will open. I open. I opened. Close.

I will close. I close. I closed. Start.

I will start. I start. I started. Stop.

I will stop. I stop. I stopped. Wait.

I will wait. I wait. I waited. Help.

I will help. I help. I helped. Show.

I will show. I show. I showed. I showed watch.

Watch. I will watch. I watch. I watched.

live. I will live. I live. I lived.

Love. I will love. I love I love I love I loved. Ask ask.

I will ask. I ask. I asked. Answer.

I will answer. I answer. I answered. Begin.

I will begin. I begin. I began. Break.

I will break. I break. I broke. Call.

I will call. I call. I called. Thank you so much for watching this video.

Don't forget to like and share it with your friends and subscribe to English Evolution Step 1 for more practical English lessons.

Frases prontas (5)

  • Every word has a job.
  • Every tense tells a story.
  • Let's turn everyday sentences into powerful learning moments.
  • I love I love I love I loved.
  • Thank you so much for watching this video.
Improve English Practice_ Real-life Sentences A1 to A2.112 falas · 0 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (112 falas)

Welcome to English Evolution Step One, your first step toward confident, practical English. In this lesson, you'll learn how one idea can grow from a short sentence to a fluent expression. From A1 to A2, we'll guide you through 100 real life sentence pairs that build your voice, your rhythm, and your confidence. I am ready.

I am ready for the exam. She is afraid. She is afraid of the dark. He is late.

He is late for school. We are friends. We are good friends at school. They are tired.

They are tired after the trip. I need help. I need help with my homework. She wants water.

She wants a glass of water. He likes music. He likes loud music. We love our parents.

We love our parents very much. They prefer coffee. They prefer coffee in the morning. They understand me.

They understand me very well. We speak slowly. We speak slowly in class. He practices English.

He practices English every day. She learns fast. She learns fast in class. I try again.

Tomorrow I try again. Tomorrow they give advice. They give advice. They give good advice.

We ask a question. We ask a question in class. He answers the phone. He answers the phone quickly.

She sends a message. She sends a message to her mother. I call my friend. I call my friend after school.

They look busy. They look busy at the office. We feel excited. We feel excited about the trip.

He seems tired. He seems tired after work. She looks happy. She looks happy today.

I feel sick. I feel sick this morning. They arrive today. They arrive today by train.

We leave early. We leave early in the morning. He comes home. He comes home late.

She goes shopping. She goes shopping with her sister. I wake up. I wake up at 6:00.

They bring food. They bring food to the picnic. We choose a gift. We choose a gift for our friend.

He pays the bill. He pays the bill at the restaurant. She sells fruit. She sells fresh fruit at the market.

I buy a book. I buy a new book. Today I buy a new book. Today they take photos.

They take photos. They take photos at the beach. We play a game. We play a game after dinner.

He draws a picture. He draws a picture of his family. She listens to music. She listens to music in her room.

I watch a movie. I watch a movie at night. They clean the house. They clean the house together.

We cook rice. We cook rice for dinner. He washes the car. He washes the car on Saturday.

She closes her bag. She closes her bag quickly. I open my book. I open my book to page 10.

They invite us. They invite us to the party. We visit grandma. We visit grandma on Sunday.

He meets his teacher. He meets his teacher after class. You've just taken your first step from A1 to A2. From short sentences to fluent expression, from simple words to real connection.

Your English is growing and your voice is getting stronger. So, don't stop here. Subscribe to English Evolution Step 1 for more poetic practical lessons. Share this video with someone who's ready to grow with you.

Comment your favorite sentence below and let's keep learning together. This is English evolution step one. Your journey has begun. Let's keep moving step by step.

  • He is late for school.

  • She wants a glass of water.
  • He likes loud music.
  • We love our parents.
  • We love our parents very much.
  • They prefer coffee in the morning.
  • They understand me very well.
  • We speak slowly in class.
  • He practices English every day.
  • She learns fast in class.
  • Tomorrow I try again.
  • Tomorrow they give advice.
  • They give good advice.
  • We ask a question.
  • We ask a question in class.
  • He answers the phone.
  • He answers the phone quickly.
  • She sends a message.
  • She sends a message to her mother.
  • I call my friend.
  • I call my friend after school.
  • They look busy at the office.
  • We feel excited about the trip.
  • He seems tired after work.
  • She looks happy today.
  • I feel sick this morning.
  • They arrive today by train.
  • We leave early in the morning.

Improve English Practice_ Real-life Sentences From A1 to A2.99 falas · 2 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (99 falas)

In this lesson, you'll learn how one idea can grow from a short sentence to a fluent expression. I need some water. I need some cold water to drink. I meet a friend.

I meet a friend after work. I greet people. I greet people with a smile. I shake hands.

I shake hands with my teacher. I ask for help. I ask for help when I need it. I give help.

I give help to my friend. I share food. I share food with my family. I invite a friend.

I invite a friend to my house. I visit a place. I visit a new place in the city. I spend time.

I spend time I spend time with my family. I spend time with my family. I enjoy the day. I enjoy the day with my friends.

I practice English. I practice English every morning. I improve slowly. I improve slowly but surely.

I understand more. I understand more with practice. I speak better. I speak better than before.

I write better. I write better every day. I read faster. I read faster now.

I listen better. I listen better in class. I learn quickly. I learn quickly with practice.

I want to learn. I want to learn more English. I try every day. I try every day to improve.

I never stop. I never stop learning. I keep going. I keep going even when it is hard.

I open a shop. I open a shop in the morning. I close the shop. I close the shop at night.

I serve customers. I serve customers every day. I sell food. I sell food at the market.

I buy goods. I buy goods for my shop. I count money. I count money at the end of the day.

I save money. I save money every month. I spend money. I spend money on food.

I earn money. I earn money from my job. I lose money. I lose money sometimes.

I wake up late. I wake up late on Sunday. I stay in bed. I stay in bed in the morning.

I get dressed. I get dressed quickly. I eat lunch. I eat lunch at noon.

I drink water. I drink water all day. I take a walk. In the evening, I take a walk.

In the evening, I go shopping. I go shopping. I go shopping on Saturday. I clean my house.

I clean my house every weekend. I wash clothes. I wash clothes on Sunday. I cook dinner.

I cook dinner every night. You've just taken your first step from A1 to A2. From short sentences to fluent expression, from simple words to real connection. Your English is growing and your voice is getting stronger.

So don't stop here. Subscribe to English Evolution Step 1 for more poetic practical lessons. Share this video with someone who's ready to grow with you. Comment your favorite sentence below and let's keep learning together.

This is English evolution step one. Your journey has begun. Let's keep moving step by step.

Expressões em contexto (2)

  • at the end of the day no fim das contas
    “I count money at the end of the day.”
  • want to querer
    “I want to learn.”

  • I need some cold water to drink.
  • I meet a friend.
  • I meet a friend after work.
  • I greet people with a smile.
  • I shake hands with my teacher.
  • I ask for help.
  • I ask for help when I need it.
  • I give help to my friend.
  • I share food with my family.
  • I invite a friend.
  • I invite a friend to my house.
  • I visit a place.
  • I visit a new place in the city.
  • I spend time I spend time with my family.
  • I spend time with my family.
  • I enjoy the day.
  • I enjoy the day with my friends.
  • I practice English every morning.
  • I improve slowly but surely.
  • I understand more with practice.
  • I speak better than before.
  • I write better every day.
  • I read faster now.
  • I listen better in class.
  • I learn quickly with practice.
  • I want to learn.
  • I want to learn more English.
  • I try every day.
  • I try every day to improve.
  • I never stop learning.
  • I keep going even when it is hard.
  • I open a shop.
  • I open a shop in the morning.
  • I close the shop.
  • I close the shop at night.

Improve English Practice_ Transportation in English.239 falas · 2 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (239 falas)

Let's learn about land transport. The vehicles we use on roads every day. Car. I go to work by car.

Bus. We wait for the bus every morning. Taxi. She takes a taxi to the hospital.

Train train. They travel to the city by train. motorbike. Motorbike He rides his motorbike to school.

He rides his motorbike to school. Bicycle. I ride my bicycle in the park. Truck.

The truck carries food to the shop. Van van. We move our things in a van. Scooter.

The child rides a scooter on the street. Subway. I take the subway to downtown. Parking parking.

The car is in the parking. The car is in the parking garage. Garage. My bike is in the garage.

Highway. We drive fast on the highway. Roundabout. The bus goes around the roundabout.

Tunnel. The train passes through the tunnel. Railway. The railway is near my house.

Platform. We wait on the platform. Ticket office. She buys a ticket at the office.

Conductor. The conductor checks the tickets. schedule schedule. The bus schedule is on the wall.

Great job. Now you know the different ways we travel on land. Next, let's explore air transport. The fastest way to travel long distances.

Airplane. We fly to Paris by airplane. Airport. The taxi stops at the airport.

Pilot pilot The pilot flies the plane safely. The pilot flies the plane safely. Passenger passenger. The passengers wait in the airplane.

Ticket. I buy a ticket for the flight. Flight flight. Our flight is at 900 a.m.

boarding pass. He shows the boarding pass. Gate gate. We wait at the gate.

Luggage. Her luggage is heavy. Check in. We check in at the airport.

Security. Security checks the bags. Departure. The departure is at 10:00.

Arrival. The arrival is late. Runway runway. The plane is on the runway.

Airline airline. The airline is very good. Well done. You've learned the words we use when flying in the sky.

Now, let's look at water transport. The boats and ships that move on rivers and seas. Boat. He goes fishing in a boat.

Ship. The ship sails across the sea. Fairy. We take the ferry to the island.

Port. The ship arrives at the port. Captain Captain The captain drives the big ship. The captain drives the big ship.

Harbor. Harbor The boat is in the harbor. The boat is in the harbor. Dock.

The ship stops at the dock. Crew. The crew works on the ship. life jacket.

I wear a life jacket. Wave. The waves are big today. Ocean.

The ship is on the ocean. River. We sail on the river. Lake.

The boat is on the lake. Canoe. He rides a canoe. Kayak.

She paddles the kayak. Excellent. You can now talk about traveling on water. Let's discover street and travel words.

The things we see when we move around the city. Road. The car is on the road. Street.

Children play in the street. Bridge. We walk across the bridge. Station station.

The train stops at the station. Stop. The bus stops at the school. Traffic.

There is heavy traffic today. Traffic light. The car stops at the red traffic light. crosswalk.

We walk on the crosswalk. Driver. The driver opens the bus door. Seat.

I sit on the bus seat. Bus stop. We wait at the bus stop. Taxi stand.

The taxi stand is near the hotel. Parking lot. The parking lot is full. Sidewalk.

We walk on the sidewalk. Corner. The bus turns at the corner. sign.

Sign The sign says stop. The sign says stop. Direction. He asks for the direction.

Path. We walk on the path. Journey time. The journey time is short.

destination. Destination Paris is our destination. Paris is our destination. Fantastic.

You know the words that help us travel safely. Now, let's learn travel actions. The verbs we use when we move from place to place. Travel.

I travel to Spain in summer. Go. We go to school by bus. Come.

She comes home by taxi. Ride ride. He rides his bike every day. Drive.

My father drives the car. Walk. We walk to the park. Run.

The boy runs to the bus. Fly, fly. Birds fly in the sky. Sail.

The boat sails on the river. Wait. We wait for the train. Catch.

I catch the bus at 8. Miss Miss We miss the train today. We miss the train today. Arrive.

They arrive at the station. Leave. The bus leaves at 7. Start.

The car starts now. Stop. The taxi stops here. Turn.

He turns left at the street. Wait for Wait for We wait for the bus. We wait for the bus. Get on.

I get on the train. Get off. She gets off the bus. Great work.

You can now describe how we travel and move. Finally, let's learn travel essentials. The important things we need for a trip. Map.

I look at the map. Bag bag. She carries a bag on the bus. Ticket ticket.

He shows the ticket to the driver. Money. We pay money for the taxi. Time.

The train comes on time. Late late. The bus is late today. Early early.

We arrive early at the station. Journey. The journey is long but nice. trip.

We take a trip to the beach. Holiday. They go on holiday by plane. Great job today.

You've taken another step forward in your English journey. Remember, practice a little every day. Small steps make big progress. Congratulations.

You've completed step one of English evolution. Now you can talk about transport, travel, and the essentials of moving around in daily life. Keep practicing these words and sentences. They will make your English stronger every day.

Remember, every step you take brings you closer to fluency. Stay confident, stay curious, and keep smiling with your English. See you in the next lesson.

Expressões em contexto (2)

  • go on continuar / prosseguir
    “They go on holiday by plane.”
  • you know sabe / entende
    “Now you know the different ways we travel on land.”

  • Let's learn about land transport.
  • The vehicles we use on roads every day.
  • I go to work by car.
  • We wait for the bus every morning.
  • She takes a taxi to the hospital.
  • They travel to the city by train.
  • Motorbike He rides his motorbike to school.
  • He rides his motorbike to school.
  • I ride my bicycle in the park.
  • The truck carries food to the shop.
  • We move our things in a van.
  • The child rides a scooter on the street.
  • I take the subway to downtown.
  • The car is in the parking.
  • The car is in the parking garage.
  • My bike is in the garage.
  • We drive fast on the highway.
  • The bus goes around the roundabout.
  • The train passes through the tunnel.
  • The railway is near my house.
  • We wait on the platform.
  • She buys a ticket at the office.
  • The conductor checks the tickets.
  • The bus schedule is on the wall.
  • Now you know the different ways we travel on land.
  • Next, let's explore air transport.
  • The fastest way to travel long distances.
  • We fly to Paris by airplane.
  • The taxi stops at the airport.
  • Pilot pilot The pilot flies the plane safely.
  • The pilot flies the plane safely.
  • The passengers wait in the airplane.
  • I buy a ticket for the flight.
  • Our flight is at 900 a.m.
  • He shows the boarding pass.
  • We wait at the gate.
  • Her luggage is heavy.
  • We check in at the airport.
  • Security checks the bags.
  • The departure is at 10:00.
Improve English Speaking Practice_ Action Verbs And Phrases.79 falas · 0 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (79 falas)

I will come. I am coming. I will go. I am going.

I will eat. I am eating. I will drink. I am drinking.

I will play. I am playing. I will visit. I am visiting.

I will learn. I am learning. I will call. I am calling.

I will write. I am writing. I will buy. I am buying.

I will work. I am working. I will meet him. I am meeting him.

I will travel. I am traveling. I will sleep. I am sleeping.

I will study. I am studying. I will talk. I am talking.

I will dance. I am dancing. I will watch TV. I am watching TV.

I will run. I am running. I will read. I am reading.

I will cook. I am cooking. I will drive. I am driving.

I will sing. I am singing. I will wait. I am waiting.

I will wash. I am washing. I will listen. I am listening.

I will change. I am changing. I will shower. I am showering.

I will clean. I am cleaning. I will draw. I am drawing.

I will build. I am building. I will break. I am breaking.

Come, go, eat, play, visit, learn, call, Right. Buy work, meet, travel, sleep, study, talk, dance, wash, Listen. Change. Shower.

Clean. Draw. Build. Break.

Watch. Run. Read. Cook.

Drive. Sing. Wait.

Frases prontas (6)

  • I will meet him.
  • I am meeting him.
  • I will watch TV.
  • I am watching TV.
  • Come, go, eat, play, visit, learn, call, Right.
  • Buy work, meet, travel, sleep, study, talk, dance, wash, Listen.
Improve English_ A1 English Prepositions & Phrasal Verbs.133 falas · 7 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (133 falas)

Prepositions, phrasal verbs in simple sentences. Listen carefully and practice. At at at school. She is at school.

In in my phone is in my bag. My phone is in my bag. on on the book is on the table. The book is on the table.

Under. Under under. The cat is under the chair. Behind.

The boy is behind the door. Between B between The shop is between the bank and the cafe. The shop is between the bank and the cafe. Next to next to the school is next to the park.

The school is next to the park. near near. We live near the beach. Above.

The lamp is above the table. below. The shoes are below the bed. across.

The bakery is across the street. Prepositions of time at. at the class starts at 9:00. The class starts at 9:00.

on on we meet on Monday. We meet on Monday in in She was born in June. She was born in June. before.

Wash your hands before dinner. after. We play football after school. during.

He slept during the movie. Since since I have lived here since 2020. I have lived here since 2020. Four.

We stayed in Paris for 2 weeks. until until Wait until 5:00. Wait until 5:00. Prepositions of movement and direction.

Two. She goes to the market. into into He walked into the room. He walked into the room.

Out of out of She ran out of the house. She ran out of the house. from from He comes from Sagal. He comes from Sagal.

Towards towards They walked towards the station. They walked towards the station. Up. He climbed up the hill.

Down. The ball rolled down the street. through through. We walked through the park.

We walked through the park with with I am with my friends. I am with my friends. By by the book is by the window. The book is by the window about about.

We talked about the movie. We talked about the movie like like like it looks like a cat. It looks like a cat. Daily routine phrasal verbs.

Wake up. I wake up at 7:00. Get up. She gets up early every day.

Sit down. Please sit down on the chair. Stand up. The students stand up when the teacher comes.

Go out. We go out on Sundays. Come back. He comes back home at 6:00.

Go in. She goes in the room. clothing and objects. Put on.

He puts on his jacket. Take off. She takes off her shoes. Pick up.

Pick up the pen from the floor. Put down. Put down put down your bag, please. Put down your bag, please.

Throw away. Don't throw away the paper. Put away. Put away the toys after playing.

Communication. Look for. I am looking for my keys. Find out.

She found out the answer. Write down. Write down Write down the new words. Write down the new words.

Call back. I will call you back later. Talk about talk about We talked about the movie. We talked about the movie.

Everyday actions. Turn on. Turn on the light, please. Turn off.

Turn off the TV. Pick up. He picked up the phone. Give back.

Give back the book to your friend. Take out. Take out the trash. come from.

Come from I come from Sagal. I come from Sagal. Social and emotional. Get on.

I get on well with my classmates. Look after. She looks after her little brother. Take care of take care of.

He takes care of his dog. Get along. We get along with our neighbors. Give up.

Don't give up learning English.

Expressões em contexto (7)

  • find out descobrir
    “Find out.”
  • give up desistir
    “Give up.”
  • look for procurar
    “Look for.”
  • pick up pegar / buscar
    “Pick up.”
  • get along se dar bem
    “Get along.”
  • take care se cuidar
    “Take care of take care of.”
  • look after cuidar de
    “Look after.”

Frases prontas (40)

  • Prepositions, phrasal verbs in simple sentences.
  • Listen carefully and practice.
  • At at at school.
  • She is at school.
  • In in my phone is in my bag.
  • My phone is in my bag.
  • on on the book is on the table.
  • The book is on the table.
  • The cat is under the chair.
  • The boy is behind the door.
  • Between B between The shop is between the bank and the cafe.
  • The shop is between the bank and the cafe.
  • Next to next to the school is next to the park.
  • The school is next to the park.
  • We live near the beach.
  • The lamp is above the table.
  • The shoes are below the bed.
  • The bakery is across the street.
  • Prepositions of time at.
  • at the class starts at 9:00.
  • The class starts at 9:00.
  • on on we meet on Monday.
  • We meet on Monday in in She was born in June.
  • She was born in June.
  • Wash your hands before dinner.
  • We play football after school.
  • He slept during the movie.
  • Since since I have lived here since 2020.
  • I have lived here since 2020.
  • We stayed in Paris for 2 weeks.
  • until until Wait until 5:00.
  • Prepositions of movement and direction.
  • She goes to the market.
  • into into He walked into the room.
  • He walked into the room.
  • Out of out of She ran out of the house.
  • She ran out of the house.
  • from from He comes from Sagal.
  • He comes from Sagal.
  • Towards towards They walked towards the station.
Improve English_ Action Verbs In English A2 Examples.83 falas · 2 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (83 falas)

Do you want to speak English in a simple clear way? Listen carefully and practice. Walk. I am walking right now to school with my friends.

run. She is running quickly in the park because the weather is nice. read. He is reading a new book in his bedroom before going to sleep.

Write. I am writing a short email to my teacher about the homework. I am writing a short email to my teacher about the homework speak. She is speaking English with her classmates during the lesson.

Listen, listen. We are listening to music in the living room after dinner. study. He is studying French at school to pass his exam.

learn. I am learning new words every day with my notebook. Ask ask. She is asking a question to the teacher during class.

Answer. He is answering the phone while he is doing homework. Eat eat. We are eating lunch together at the small restaurant near the market.

Drink. They are drinking cold water after playing football in the sun. Cook. She is cooking rice and fish for her family tonight.

Clean clean. We are cleaning the kitchen after dinner. Open. He is opening the window because the room is hot.

Close. I am closing the door quietly so the baby can sleep. Sit. She is sitting on the chair near the table.

Stand. He is standing at the bus stop waiting for the bus. Sleep. They are sleeping 8 hours every night to stay healthy.

Wake up. I am waking up early to prepare for school. By She is buying fresh fruit at the market this morning. She is buying fresh fruit at the market this morning.

Sell sell. He is selling clothes in the shop downtown. Pay pay. We are paying for the tickets at the cinema.

Take They are taking the bus to the city center. They are taking the bus to the city center. Give give I am giving a gift to my best friend today. I am giving a gift to my best friend today.

Get get. She is getting a letter from her cousin in France. Put put He is putting the book on the table carefully. He is putting the book on the table carefully.

Carry carry. We are carrying heavy bags upstairs together. Bring They are bringing food to the party tonight. They are bringing food to the party tonight.

Hold. I am holding my baby in my arms. Drive. He is driving to work in his car.

Ride ride. I am riding my bicycle to the park. Wait, wait. She is waiting for the bus at the station.

Look, look. We are looking at the stars in the sky. find. They are finding money on the street.

Lose. He is losing his keys again this morning. call. Call I am calling my friends to meet at the cafe.

I am calling my friends to meet at the cafe. visit visit. She is visiting her grandparents this weekend. Travel travel.

We are traveling to Spain for our holiday. Stay stay. They are staying at home today because it is raining.

Expressões em contexto (2)

  • right now agora mesmo
    “I am walking right now to school with my friends.”
  • want to querer
    “Do you want to speak English in a simple clear way?”

Frases prontas (40)

  • Do you want to speak English in a simple clear way?
  • Listen carefully and practice.
  • I am walking right now to school with my friends.
  • She is running quickly in the park because the weather is nice.
  • He is reading a new book in his bedroom before going to sleep.
  • I am writing a short email to my teacher about the homework.
  • I am writing a short email to my teacher about the homework speak.
  • She is speaking English with her classmates during the lesson.
  • We are listening to music in the living room after dinner.
  • He is studying French at school to pass his exam.
  • I am learning new words every day with my notebook.
  • She is asking a question to the teacher during class.
  • He is answering the phone while he is doing homework.
  • We are eating lunch together at the small restaurant near the market.
  • They are drinking cold water after playing football in the sun.
  • She is cooking rice and fish for her family tonight.
  • We are cleaning the kitchen after dinner.
  • He is opening the window because the room is hot.
  • I am closing the door quietly so the baby can sleep.
  • She is sitting on the chair near the table.
  • He is standing at the bus stop waiting for the bus.
  • They are sleeping 8 hours every night to stay healthy.
  • I am waking up early to prepare for school.
  • By She is buying fresh fruit at the market this morning.
  • She is buying fresh fruit at the market this morning.
  • He is selling clothes in the shop downtown.
  • We are paying for the tickets at the cinema.
  • Take They are taking the bus to the city center.
  • They are taking the bus to the city center.
  • Give give I am giving a gift to my best friend today.
  • I am giving a gift to my best friend today.
  • She is getting a letter from her cousin in France.
  • Put put He is putting the book on the table carefully.
  • He is putting the book on the table carefully.
  • We are carrying heavy bags upstairs together.
  • Bring They are bringing food to the party tonight.
  • They are bringing food to the party tonight.
  • I am holding my baby in my arms.
  • He is driving to work in his car.
  • I am riding my bicycle to the park.
Improve English_ Action Verbs With Pronouns in English A1.310 falas · 0 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (310 falas)

English verbs training with personal pronouns in action. Drive. I drive to work. We drive together.

You drive well. They drive fast. He drives a taxi. She drives every day.

Ride ride. I ride my bike. We ride horses. You ride the bus.

They ride scooters. He rides a motorcycle. She rides with her friend. Fly fly.

I fly to Paris. We fly in a plane. You fly tomorrow. They fly often.

He flies for work. She flies with her family. Wait. I wait for the bus.

We wait in line. You wait here. They wait outside. He waits for his friend.

She waits at the door. Leave. I leave at 8. We leave together.

You leave now. They leave early. He leaves the house. She leaves her bag.

Arrive. I arrive on time. We arrive at school. You arrive late.

They arrive tomorrow. He arrives at work. She arrives with a smile. Travel.

I travel a lot. We travel by train. You travel for fun. They travel every summer.

He travels for business. She travels with her sister. Turn. I turn left.

We turn the page. You turn the light on. They turn around. He turns the key.

She turns slowly. Cross cross. I cross the street. We cross the bridge.

You cross here. They cross together. He crosses the road. She crosses with care.

Love. I love music. We love our family. You love chocolate.

They love to dance. He loves his dog. She loves flowers. Hate.

I hate noise. We hate waiting. You hate cold weather. They hate traffic.

He hates onions. She hates being late. Smile. I smile at you.

We smile in photos. You smile every day. They smile together. He smiles at the baby.

She smiles with joy. Cry. I cry sometimes. We cry during movies.

You cry easily. They cry at night. He cries when he's sad. She cries quietly.

laugh. I laugh with friends. We laugh a lot. You laugh loudly.

They laugh at jokes. He laughs at the story. She laughs with her sister. Hug.

I hug my mom. We hug each other. You hug your friends. You hug your friend.

They hug before leaving. He hugs his son. She hugs her grandma. Kiss kiss.

I kiss my baby. We kiss goodbye. You kiss your child. They kiss on the cheek.

He kisses her hand. She kisses the baby. Meet me. I meet new people.

We meet at the cafe. You meet your teacher. They meet every week. He meets his friend.

She meets her cousin. Call I call my brother. I call my brother. We call the doctor.

You call now. They call every day. He calls his boss. She calls her mom.

Visit. I visit my aunt. We visit the museum. You visit often.

They visit their friends. He visits his hometown. She visits her uncle. Phone.

I phone my dad. We phone the office. You phone your friend. They phone every evening.

He phones his teacher. She phones her sister. Text. I text my friend.

We text each other. You text quickly. They text all day. He texts his mom.

She texts her cousin. Email email. I email my teacher. We email the school.

You email your boss. They email the company. He emails his friend. She emails her manager.

Send. I send a message. We send gifts. You send the file.

They send money. He sends a photo. She sends a letter. Search.

I search online. We search for answers. You search well. They search the house.

He searches the room. She searches for her keys. Click. I click the link.

We click together. You click too fast. They click on the video. He clicks the button.

She clicks the icon. Type. I type my name. We type the report.

You type quickly. They type on laptops. He types the email. She types her story.

Post. I post a photo. We post online. You post every day.

They post comments. He posts news. She posts her art. Share.

I share my food. We share ideas. You share your story. They share everything.

He shares his book. She shares her smile. Watch. I watch a video.

We watch together. You watch the show. They watch cartoons. He watches the clip.

She watches quietly. House. I live in a house. We clean the house.

You like this house. They build houses. He paints the house. She enters the house.

Kitchen. I cook in the kitchen. We clean the kitchen. You love your kitchen.

They eat in the kitchen. He walks into the kitchen. She sings in the kitchen. bed.

Bed I sleep in my bed. I sleep in my bed. We make the bed. You jump on the bed.

They lie on the bed. He rests in bed. She reads in bed. Chair.

I sit on the chair. We move the chairs. You fix the chair. They carry chairs.

He paints the chair. She chooses a chair. Table. I clean the table.

We eat at the table. You set the table. They move the table. He builds a table.

She decorates the table. Door. I open the door. We close the door.

You knock on the door. They fix the door. He locks the door. She paints the door.

Cheap. I buy cheap shoes. We like cheap food. You find cheap clothes.

They sell cheap books. He chooses the cheap one. She wants something cheap. Expensive.

I avoid expensive things. We skip expensive places. You buy expensive gifts. They sell expensive phones.

He wears expensive shoes. She likes expensive perfume. phone. I use my phone.

We charge the phone. You drop your phone. They call on the phone. He checks his phone.

She answers the phone. Video. I watch a video. We record a video.

You send the video. They post the video. He edits the video. She shares the video.

Friend. I call my friend. We meet our friend. You help your friend.

They visit their friend. He hugs his friend. She talks to her friend. Family.

I love my family. We live with family. You call your family. They support their family.

He misses his family. She cooks for her family. Happy. I feel happy today.

We are happy together. You look happy. They seem happy. He is happy now.

She stays happy always. Sad. I feel sad sometimes. We are sad today.

You look sad. They feel sad, too. He is sad about the news. She seems sad and quiet.

Thank you. You've just taken one more step toward real English, real confidence, and real connection. From morning greetings to daily routines, every sentence brings you closer to fluency that feels natural and true. Keep listening, keep practicing, keep feeling.

Your journey is just beginning, and every word you speak is a seed of growth. And don't forget to subscribe and like English Evolution Step One for more practical English lessons.

Frases prontas (40)

  • English verbs training with personal pronouns in action.
  • I drive to work.
  • He drives a taxi.
  • She drives every day.
  • I ride my bike.
  • You ride the bus.
  • He rides a motorcycle.
  • She rides with her friend.
  • I fly to Paris.
  • We fly in a plane.
  • He flies for work.
  • She flies with her family.
  • I wait for the bus.
  • We wait in line.
  • He waits for his friend.
  • She waits at the door.
  • I leave at 8.
  • He leaves the house.
  • She leaves her bag.
  • I arrive on time.
  • We arrive at school.
  • He arrives at work.
  • She arrives with a smile.
  • I travel a lot.
  • We travel by train.
  • You travel for fun.
  • They travel every summer.
  • He travels for business.
  • She travels with her sister.
  • We turn the page.
  • You turn the light on.
  • He turns the key.
  • I cross the street.
  • We cross the bridge.
  • He crosses the road.
  • She crosses with care.
  • We love our family.
  • They love to dance.
  • He loves his dog.
  • You hate cold weather.
Personal Pronouns In English Grammar for (A1–C2).309 falas · 1 expr

Diálogo — conversa real (309 falas)

What if one word could change how you see yourself, how you speak, how you connect, how you belong? These aren't just pronouns. Their power, their presence, their poetry. From beginner to mastery.

This is your journey through personal pronouns. Six levels, five types, one voice. Yours. Let's begin.

Personal pronouns in English. Here are six levels. A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2. We will dive into subject pronouns, object pronouns, possessive pronouns, possessive adjectives, and reflexive pronouns in the easiest order, step by step.

Girl, friends, couple man, woman, computer, cat. Welcome to the beginning. A1, beginner. Here we meet the words that help us speak about ourselves and others.

Simple, clear, friendly. Let's learn how to say I, we, you, they, he, she, and, it. Let's look at these examples with pronouns. Subject pronouns.

I am tired. We are ready. You are nice. They are here.

He is tall. She is happy. It is cold. Object pronouns.

Help me. Call us. I see you. Watch them.

Meet him. Ask her. Move it. Possessive adjectives.

My book. Our house. Your pen. Their shoes.

his phone. Her dress. its name. Possessive pronouns.

This is mine. That is ours. Is it yours? These are theirs.

That's his. The bag is hers. The toy is its. The toy is its reflexive pronouns.

I hurt myself. We made it ourselves. You did it yourself. They helped themselves.

He washed himself. She dressed herself. It cleaned itself. You've just taken your first step.

Now you can talk about people, things, and yourself with confidence. Let's keep going. The journey has only begun. A2 high beginner.

Now we go a little further. You already know the basics. Now let's use them in real life. Shopping.

talking, helping, sharing. Let's make your English more natural and useful. Subject pronouns. I go shopping on Fridays.

We play music together. You speak clearly. They walk to school. He works at the bank.

She likes dancing. It looks old. Object pronouns. He called me yesterday.

The teacher gave us homework. I saw you at the cafe. I met them last week. We invited him.

I helped her with her bag. Can you move it? Possessive adjectives. My keys are missing.

Our school is big. Your jacket is here. Their books are new. His room is clean.

Her phone is broken. Its color is bright. possessive pronouns. That phone is mine.

The house is ours. Is this umbrella yours? The shoes are theirs. The pen is his.

The dress is hers. The food is its The food is its reflexive pronouns. I made tea by myself. We cooked dinner ourselves.

You can do it yourself. They built the house themselves. He fixed the bike himself. She washed herself.

The cat cleaned itself. Well done. You've added more detail, more rhythm, more life to your words. Next, we'll explore how to express ideas with even more clarity.

B1 intermediate. Welcome to the middle ground. You're no longer just learning, you're communicating. Let's use personal pronouns in emails, meetings, and everyday stories.

This is where your English starts to feel like your own. Subject pronouns. I usually check my emails first. We've joined a new club.

You always bring good ideas. They arrived late but apologized. He travels often for work. She enjoys reading novels.

It seems the weather is changing. Object pronouns. The manager asked me to help. The coach gave us feedback.

I didn't see you at the event. The team invited them to speak. We saw him at the meeting. I met her at the conference.

Can you install it today? Possessive adjectives. I forgot my password. We're updating our website.

Is this your presentation? They brought their own tools. He lost his phone. She shared her notes.

The company changed its policy. Possessive pronouns. That laptop is mine. The tickets are ours.

Is this notebook yours? These seats are theirs. The decision is his. The necklace is hers.

The fault is its. The fault is its reflexive pronouns. I taught myself how to edit videos. We challenged ourselves to improve.

You should trust yourself more. They organized the event themselves. He blamed himself for the mistake. She reminded herself to stay calm.

The robot repaired itself. You've reached a new level of fluency. Your sentences are smoother. Your meaning is clearer.

Let's keep building with confidence and creativity. B2 upper intermediate. Now we speak with nuance. You're ready to express opinions, make decisions, and explain yourself.

Personal pronouns help you sound natural, professional, and precise. Let's refine your voice. Subject pronouns. I've been thinking about changing careers.

We submitted the proposal yesterday. You seem more confident lately. They promised to send the files. He rarely checks his messages.

She's been writing a novel. It appears the system needs an update. Object pronouns. The director asked me to lead the session.

The manager gave us feedback. I didn't recognize you at first. The board congratulated them. We invited him to the panel.

I consulted her for advice. Can you restart it before noon? Possessive adjectives. I updated my resume.

We revised our goals. You refined your approach. They presented their findings. He reshaped his career.

She refined her message. The software changed its settings. Possessive pronouns. That idea was mine.

The final decision is ours. Is this seat yours? The credit is theirs. The responsibility is his.

The award is hers. The error is its. The error is its reflexive pronouns. I challenged myself to grow.

We prepared ourselves for the interview. You should believe in yourself. They proved themselves capable. He taught himself to code.

She reminded herself to stay focused. The system updated itself automatically. You've mastered the art of clarity. Your English now fits the workplace, the classroom, and the world.

Let's move toward elegance and depth. C1 advanced. Welcome to the world of expression. Here, every word carries weight.

Personal pronouns become tools of style, tone, and subtle meaning. Let's speak with grace and intention. Subject pronouns. I often reflect on past decisions.

We carry ourselves with quiet strength. You navigate complexity with grace. They revealed their true intentions. He reinvented himself after failure.

She expressed herself with clarity. It revealed unexpected patterns. Object pronouns. They entrusted me with the final call.

The world offered us a challenge. I admire you for your resilience. History remembers them. Time tested him.

I consulted her for insight. We examined it thoroughly. Possessive adjectives. I reshaped my perspective.

We refined our strategy. You adjusted your tone. They redefined their roles. He revised his goals.

She clarified her position. It adjusted its behavior. Possessive pronouns. The legacy is mine.

The outcome is ours. The choice is yours. The story is theirs. The burden is his.

The achievement is hers. The flaw is its. The flaw is its reflexive pronouns. I found myself drawn to silence.

We challenged ourselves to evolve. You define yourself through action. They proved themselves under pressure. He forgave himself and moved forward.

She reminded herself of her strength. It corrected itself without help. You've entered the realm of fluent thought. Your English now reflects your ideas, your emotions, your identity.

Let's take one final step into mastery. C2, mastery. This is the final level. Here, language becomes art.

Personal pronouns help you reflect, persuade, and connect with depth and elegance. Let's speak like a storyteller, a thinker, a leader. Subject pronouns. I find myself drawn to silence when words fail.

We carry the weight of memory and meaning. You shape reality with your choices. They speak through action, not words. He walks through failure with grace.

She moves with quiet certainty. It echoes through time. Object pronouns. They entrusted me with legacy.

The world challenged us to grow. I see you becoming who you are. History shaped them. time tested him.

I listened to her wisdom. we studied it deeply. Possessive adjectives. I carry my truth.

We honor our past. You follow your path. They hold their story. He owns his mistakes.

She speaks her truth. It holds its meaning. Possessive pronouns. The silence is mine.

The journey is ours. The decision is yours. The memory is theirs. The burden is his.

The triumph is hers. The mystery is its. The mystery is its reflexive pronouns. I reminded myself to breathe.

We found ourselves in the moment. You taught yourself to rise. They proved themselves through silence. He forgave himself completely.

She trusted herself again. It revealed itself in time. You have completed the journey from simple words to powerful expression. Now your English is not only correct, it's alive.

Keep speaking, keep growing, keep inspiring. This is English evolution. This is smart English reader and this is just the beginning. So stay tuned, stay curious and keep evolving.

One scene, one sentence, one step at a time.

Expressões em contexto (1)

  • feel like estar a fim / parecer
    “This is where your English starts to feel like your own.”

  • Now we go a little further.

  • I saw you at the cafe.
  • I met them last week.

  • My keys are missing.
  • Our school is big.
  • Your jacket is here.
  • Their books are new.
  • His room is clean.

lesson one greetings dialogue one good morning good morning to you too do you think it will rain today yeah maybe it's hard to tell I hope not I am going to the beach this afternoon maybe you should that sounds good to me dialogue five good afternoon is Dr Colin yes but he is busy do you mind waiting I don't mind do you think he will be a while he shouldn't be long oh good I'll wait just take a seat over there he should see you Introductions shortly lesson two introductions dialogue one hi I'm Marvin Marvin my name is Robert I'm here to play baseball good we need more people to play I'm not Gratitude dialogues three gratitude dialogue one Eric did you finish the report yes it's right here Mark thanks no problem were there any difficulties for you it was tough to get it done promptly but it was not too bad that's good to hear keep up the good work dialogue 2 I thought you might like this book oh wow yeah this is great don't mention it no this is great this is just what I wanted thank you well you're welcome she is my favorite author I'm glad you like it dialogue three you do fine work here oh I just tried to do my part no you do better than that and we appreciate it really I'm
